item.title: 这是令人兴奋的：所有事物 DevOps 的新目的地 | This is Exciting: A New Destination for All Things DevOps
item.description:
我很高兴能重点介绍一个新的 Forem： Ops 社区。 这是一个专门为 DevOps、SecOps、IT 经理、SRE 和其他云原生专业人士打造的新空间。

如果您从事 DevOps 工作，您就会知道这是一个快速发展的领域，紧跟潮流是至关重要的——但这可能很困难，而且势不可挡。 无论好信息会在大量 JavaScript 教程中丢失，还是仅限于聊天空间和 Twitter 线程，将信号与噪音分开对您的职业生涯至关重要，并且需要更容易一些。

我们昨天邀请了一些人加入，这个空间已经很繁荣了。

如果您是一名全面的 DevOps 专业人士，或者只是倾向于 IT 领域的那个方向，那么这对您来说将是一个非常有用的资源和社区。 请花点时间立即加入并在欢迎帖中发帖。

感谢Blink管理这个社区！

快乐的操作！ 最后一件事。 您会注意到“使用 Forem 注册”作为这些 Forem 的一个选项。 这是我们现在提供的一项服务，可以最无缝地导航生态系统并管理您的身份。 我们希望您能够在无需携带全部数据的情况下充分发挥自我，因此从长远来看，使这个分布式生态系统尽可能简单是非常重要的。 创建一个Forem 帐户并通过您的设置将其与您的 DEV 帐户连接。 🙂 🙂



原文:

I am very excited to highlight a new Forem: The Ops Community. It is a new space dedicated to DevOps, SecOps, IT Managers, SREs, and other cloud-native professionals.

If you work in DevOps, you know that it is a fast-moving field where keeping up with the pulse is critical — but that can be difficult and overwhelming. Whether good information gets lost in a sea of JavaScript tutorials or is confined to chat spaces and Twitter threads, separating signal from noise is critical for your career and needs to be just a bit easier.

We invited some folks yesterday to join, and the space is already thriving.

The Ops Community is a place for cloud engineers of all experience levels to share tips & tricks, tutorials, and career insights.

If you are a full-on DevOps professional, or simply trend in that direction of the IT spectrum, this is going to be a very useful resource and community for you. Please take a moment to join right now and post in the Welcome Thread.

Thank you to Blink for stewarding this community!

Happy Opsing! 🎉

One last thing. You’ll notice "Sign up with Forem" as an option on these Forems. This is a service we now offer to most seamlessly navigate the ecosystem and manage your identity. We want you to be able to bring your full self without having to bring your full data, so making this distributed ecosystem as straightforward as possible in the long run is really important. Create a Forem account and connect it with your DEV account via your settings. 🙂
item.author: ben

item.title: 人们认为 Web 开发人员所做的与我们实际所做的 | What people think that web developers do vs. what we really do
item.description:
克里斯蒂安·海尔曼

发表于编码、部署和使用，编码是主要任务

编码
  • 需要随机的 3rd 方框架代码
  • 更新节点/NPM
  • 使用sudo再次尝试最后一个命令
  • 在手机上检查
  • 向库比蒂诺挥舞着拳头
  • 在网上查资料
  • 粘贴堆栈溢出解决方案
  • 调整浏览器大小/使用仿真
  • 发现浏览器扩展一直是问题所在
  • 再次解释事情不可能在任何地方看起来都一样
  • 添加 ARIA 直到一切正常
  • 将性能问题追溯到分析/广告代码
  • 将内容转换为可用格式
  • 添加/删除 console.log()
  • 使缓存无效
  • 使用
  • 使用血魔法召唤 Imhotep 以帮助解决渲染错误

    • Sharifuzzaman Nakib - Sharifuzaman Nakib - Mar 3 '21 21 年 3 月 3 日



    原文:

    Christian Heilmann

    Posted on May 27

    • Code, deploy and use, with coding being the main task

    What web developers really do:

    • Coding
    • Require random 3rd party framework code
    • Updating Node/NPM
    • Try the last command again with sudo
    • Checking on mobile
    • Shaking fist towards Cupertino
    • Looking things up on the web
    • Pasting Stack Overflow solutions
    • Resize the browser/use emulation
    • Discover that a browser extension was the problem all along
    • Explain again that things can't look the same everywhere
    • Add ARIA till everything is fine
    • Trace performance issues back to analytics/ad code
    • Convert content to usable formats
    • Adding/Removing console.log()
    • Invalidate caches
    • Using
    • Summon Imhotep using blood magic to help with a rendering bug

    Sharifuzzaman Nakib - Mar 3 '21
item.author: codepo8

item.title: AWS 开源新闻和更新，#114 | AWS open source news and updates, #114
item.description:
欢迎阅读 AWS 开源通讯 #114。 本周新的开源项目包含各种与社区相关的项目，例如“instance-scheduler”一个帮助您调度 AWS 资源的工具、“libaws”一个帮助您简化创建和删除一些 AWS 资源的固执己见的工具、“elasticspot”一个很好的工具，可以帮助您重新分配弹性 IP，如果您使用的是 AWS Organizations，“auto-close-aws-accounts”可以让您关闭 AWS 账户。 我们还有许多其他项目，例如“amazon-ec2-spot-interrupter”，这是一个很好的工具，它提供了一个命令行工具来触发现场中断通知，还有其他可以帮助您使用 AWS Glue、简化 SageMaker 笔记本部署等的项目.

还精选了涵盖 Redis、DAMON、Magento、WireGuard、Amazon Linux 2、AWS Distro for OpenTelemetry、Grafana、Prometheus、Lustre、Open 3D Engine、cdk-nag、Flutter、AWS Amplify、Steampipe 和 OpenSearch 等主题的博客文章。 本周精选视频涵盖了对 AWS Lambda Powertools 的超级深入了解以及对 Kuma 的了解。 不知道Kuma是什么？ 好吧，前往视频并查看。

在深入研究之前，请查看有关 AWS 开发工具包中对 Node.js 12.x 版本的支持终止的重要通知。

宣布 AWS SDK for JavaScript (v3) 中对 Node.js 12.x 的支持结束

从 2022 年 11 月 1 日开始，AWS SDK For JavaScript (v3) 将不再支持 Node.js 12.x，该版本于 2022 年 4 月 30 日 EOL。如果您使用 Node，请务必查看帖子宣布终止对AWS SDK for JavaScript (v3) 中的 Node.js 12.x一起了解更多详细信息。

庆祝开源贡献者

本系列中发布的文章只有在贡献者和项目维护者的帮助下才有可能出现，因此我想大声疾呼并感谢那些真正为开源提供动力并让我们所有人都能够在他们所创造的基础上进行构建的人。

感谢以下开源英雄：Nick Figgins、Allen Samuels、Joe Travaglini、Joe Hu、SeongJae Park、Abhishek Gupta、Kat Morgan、Oleksiy Volkov、Arun Donti、Abdallah Shaban、Ashish Nanda、Raajhesh Kannaa Chidambaram、Marcia Villalba、 Sara Gerion、Charlotte Henkle、Nicholas Knize、James McIntyre、Mohammad Qureshi 和 Sean Zheng。

确保您找到并关注这些建设者，并及时了解他们的开源项目和贡献。

最新的开源项目

社区

实例调度程序

他整理了这篇博文，使用 Lambda、DynamoDB、API Gateway、Cognito、S3 和 CloudFront 在无服务器上创建实例调度程序来帮助您入门。

库

在 YAML 中定义您需要的内容，然后您可以使用 cli 轻松构建和部署。 用 Go 编写，检查一下，看看这是否有助于简化您创建和删除基础设施的方式。

弹性斑点

自动关闭 AWS 帐户

工具

有关此工具如何工作的详细使用指南，这是您应该添加到工具包中的工具。

此存储库包含awsglue ，这是一个 Python 库，您可以使用它来创作 AWS Glue 流 ETL 作业，该作业使用流 ETL 工作流的其他数据类型和操作扩展 Apache Spark，并bin一些允许您在本地运行 Python 库或打开的可执行文件启动 PySpark shell 以交互方式运行 Glue Spark 代码。

审计插件-for-mysql

确保你阅读了关于这个 repo 的贡献和路线图的 README。

您的代码在 Amazon Linux 环境中运行，其中包括来自您管理的 AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) 角色的 AWS 凭证。 使用事件桥和 lambda 自动售货机 Sagemaker 笔记本

演示、样品和研讨会

此存储库包含所有源代码和研讨会材料，可帮助您进行通话录音、回电、通话桥接、构建语音聊天机器人、通话转录等操作。 您可以从这里开始您的旅程。

在 GitLab Kubernetes Agent 可用之前，GitLab 集群集成是通过 SSL 连接到集群的 Kubernetes Control API 端点完成的。 所有集群部署都发生在 CI 推送到集群 API 端点的过程中。

着陆区加速器 on-aws

登陆区是一个云环境，它提供了一个推荐的起点，包括默认账户、账户结构、网络和安全布局等。 从登陆区域，您可以部署利用您的解决方案和应用程序的工作负载。

我的同事 Abhishek Gupta 在使用 AWS App Runner、Redis 和 AWS CDK 构建云原生应用程序一文中整理了一份随附指南，以引导您了解其工作原理

开放搜索

对于对 OpenSearch 感兴趣的人们来说这是个好消息，因为 v2 于本周早些时候正式发布。 蒸汽管

继上一篇博文之后，Bob Tordella 在他的博文How to perform a security audit of your AWS account in AWS CloudShell [hands on] 中向您展示了如何在 AWS CloudShell 中运行安全审计。

达蒙

我在#86中介绍了一个名为 damo 的项目，它是 DAMON 的用户空间工具（Linux 内核的数据访问监控框架子系统）。 使用它，您可以监控系统或工作负载的数据访问模式，并进行数据访问感知内存管理优化。 我最近发现这个项目是由 Amazon SeongJae Park 的某个人创建的。 您可以通过查看DAMON：数据访问监视器来了解最新更新

雷迪斯

本周早些时候宣布，Redis 应用程序开发人员现在在 ElastiCache 和 MemoryDB 中原生支持 JavaScript 对象表示法 (JSON)。 在这篇博文中，使用 ElastiCache 和 MemoryDB 解锁 JSON 工作负载Allen Samuels、Joe Travaglini 和 Joe Hu 介绍了 JSON 支持如何在技术层面上发挥作用，并提供了如何编写 JSON 文档、有效获取或设置 JSON 文档部分的示例在 Redis 中。 反相代理

在博文中，使用反向流量代理实现轻量级本地 API 连接Oleksiy Volkov 探讨了使用轻量级应用程序反向代理作为多点混合或多云、API 级连接的解决方案，适用于 AWS Direct Connect 或 VPN 的情况可能不实用。 这非常有趣，我想知道我还能如何使用这个工具。 本周必读！ 规则集 (NagPacks) 包括 HIPAA 安全性、NIST 800-53 rev 4、NIST 800-53 rev 5 和 PCI DSS 3.2.1 等内容。 在博文中，使用 AWS 云开发工具包和 cdk-nag 管理应用程序安全性和合规性Arun Donti 演示了如何将 cdk-nag 集成到 AWS CDK 应用程序中，以提供持续的反馈并帮助您的应用程序与最佳实践保持一致。 上周您可能喜欢的其他帖子

实例探究

快速更新

打开 3D 引擎

本周早些时候发布了 Open 3D Engine (O3DE) 22.05.0，这是一个 Apache 2.0 许可的开源项目。 在我们对 O3DE 的持续支持中，AWS 游戏引擎和开发人员服务团队为版本 22.05.0 贡献了许多功能，包括：

在公告中阅读有关此版本的更多信息， AWS for Games 对开放 3D 引擎 (O3DE) 的最新贡献

用于 Lustre 的 Amazon FSx

此管理功能在 FSx for Lustre 提供的当前基于网络的访问控制和 POSIX 文件权限之上添加了额外的文件访问控制层。 使用 root squash 功能，您可以限制以 root 身份访问 FSx for Lustre 文件系统的客户端的 root 级别文件系统访问。

但是，对于附加到基于 Linux 的文件系统的实例，例如 FSx for Lustre，被授予 root 访问权限的用户也可以完全访问文件系统上的文件，这在某些情况下可能并不理想。 启用 root squash 后，您可以继续为用户授予对附加到 FSx for Lustre 文件系统的实例的 root 访问权限，同时将文件系统权限限制为权限较低的用户和组的权限。

雷迪斯

通过此次发布，应用程序开发人员可以毫不费力地在 Redis 中存储、获取和更新他们的 JSON 数据，而无需管理用于序列化和反序列化的自定义代码。 使用 ElastiCache 和 MemoryDB，您现在可以高效地检索和更新 JSON 文档的特定部分，而无需操作整个对象，这有助于提高性能并降低成本。 您还可以使用 JSONPath 查询语法搜索您的 JSON 文档内容。 格拉法纳

借助 CreateWorkspaceApiKey，客户无需登录 Grafana 工作区控制台即可创建 Grafana API 令牌，使用户能够以编程方式创建、删除和管理 Grafana 资源，例如仪表板、警报和数据源。 现有和新的 Amazon Managed Grafana 工作区现在支持 Grafana 8.4 版，用户无需执行任何操作。 客户现在可以标记 Amazon Managed Grafana 工作区，以帮助简化工作区的组织和成本管理。 标签是键值对形式的标签，可以附加到 Amazon Managed Grafana 工作区以搜索、过滤或分配成本。

适用于 OpenTelemetry 的 AWS Distro

本周早些时候宣布了 AWS Distro for OpenTelemetry (ADOT) 指标的全面可用性，这是 OpenTelemetry 项目的安全、生产就绪、AWS 支持的发行版。 通过此次发布，客户可以使用 Java、.Net 和 JavaScript 中的 OpenTelemetry API 和 SDK 来收集指标并将其发送到 Amazon CloudWatch、Amazon Managed Service for Prometheus 以及 OpenTelemetry 协议 (OTLP) 支持的其他监控目标。 作为云原生计算基金会 (CNCF) 的一部分，OpenTelemetry 提供开源 API、库和代理来收集分布式跟踪和指标以用于应用程序和基础设施监控。 使用 ADOT，您只需检测一次应用程序即可将指标和跟踪发送到多个监控解决方案，并使用自动检测代理来收集跟踪和指标，而无需更改代码。 使用 AWS Distro for OpenTelemetry 检测您在 Amazon Elastic Compute Cloud (EC2)、Amazon Elastic Container Service (ECS) 和 Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (EKS) 上运行的应用程序。

对于您的可观察性需求，您可以选择 Amazon CloudWatch、Amazon Managed Service for Prometheus 或 AWS 合作伙伴目的地之一。 您可以使用 AWS CloudFormation 模板、AWS 命令​​行界面、Kubectl 命令或 ADOT EKS 插件为容器服务和 Amazon EC2 配置和部署最新版本的 AWS Distro for OpenTelemetry。 开发人员可以使用多个受支持的接收器（例如 Prometheus 和 OpenTelemetry SDK）来收集或检测他们的应用程序以收集相关的指标和跟踪。

本周视频

与 Marcia Villalba 和 Sara Gerion 一起深入研究这个项目，亲自动手并向您展示有关此工具的更多信息。 隈研吾

在此视频中，Kat Morgan 将向您介绍如何在 AWS ECS 上安装和运行 Kuma

你的日记事件

上海证券交易所 2022
自 2000 年以来，生物信息学开源会议 (BOSC) 每年举行一次，今年 AWS 很荣幸成为该活动的白金赞助商。 这是一个混合活动（面对面/虚拟），您可以通过查看活动页面BOSC 2022了解更多信息

开放搜索
每隔一个星期二，格林威治标准时间下午 3 点

此定期聚会面向对 OpenSearch 和 Open Distro 感兴趣的任何人。 欢迎所有技能水平，他们涵盖并欢迎讨论以下主题：搜索、日志记录、日志分析和数据可视化。

报名参加下一个会议， OpenSearch 社区会议

来参加第一届年度 OpenSearchCon！

这个为期一天的会议将挤满使用 OpenSearch 进行构建和创新的演讲者。 无论您是刚刚开始 OpenSearch 之旅、运行大型集群还是贡献大量代码，都没有关系； 该活动适合所有人。 加入我们，庆祝项目的进展并展望项目的未来。 入场免费，注册将在接下来的几周内开放。 您需要做的就是注册，然后前往西雅图！

在此处的聚会页面上查看完整的详细信息，包括注册和位置。

与 AWS 的开源保持联系

我希望这个总结有用。 记得查看开源主页，通过在@AWSOpen上关注我们，了解我们在开源方面的所有活动。



原文:

Welcome to the AWS open source newsletter #114. This weeks new open source projects feature a variety of community related projects such as "instance-scheduler" a tool to help you schedule AWS resources, "libaws" an opinionated tool that helps you simplify creation and deletion of some AWS resources, "elasticspot" a nice tool to help you reassign elastic IPs, and "auto-close-aws-accounts" that allows you to close AWS accounts if you are using AWS Organisations. We have plenty of other projects such as "amazon-ec2-spot-interrupter" a nice tool that provides a command line tool to trigger spot interruption notifications, and others that help you work with AWS Glue, simplify deployments of SageMaker notebooks, and others.

Also featured are blog posts covering topics such as Redis, DAMON, Magento, WireGuard, Amazon Linux 2, AWS Distro for OpenTelemetry, Grafana, Prometheus, Lustre, Open 3D Engine, cdk-nag, Flutter, AWS Amplify, Steampipe, and OpenSearch. This weeks featured videos covers a super nice deep dive on AWS Lambda Powertools and a look at Kuma. Don't know what Kuma is? Well head down to the Videos and check it out.

Before diving in, check out this important notice about the end of support for the Node.js 12.x release in the AWS SDK.

Announcing the end of support for Node.js 12.x in the AWS SDK for JavaScript (v3)

Starting November 1, 2022, AWS SDK For JavaScript (v3) will no longer support Node.js 12.x which was EOL on April 30, 2022. If you use Node, make sure you check out the post Announcing the end of support for Node.js 12.x in the AWS SDK for JavaScript (v3) for more details together.

Celebrating open source contributors

The articles posted in this series are only possible thanks to contributors and project maintainers and so I would like to shout out and thank those folks who really do power open source and enable us all to build on top of what they have created.

So thank you to the following open source heroes: Nick Figgins, Allen Samuels, Joe Travaglini, Joe Hu, SeongJae Park, Abhishek Gupta, Kat Morgan, Oleksiy Volkov, Arun Donti, Abdallah Shaban, Ashish Nanda, Raajhesh Kannaa Chidambaram, Marcia Villalba, Sara Gerion, Charlotte Henkle, Nicholas Knize, James McIntyre, Mohammad Qureshi, and Sean Zheng.

Make sure you find and follow these builders and keep up to date with their open source projects and contributions.

Latest open source projects

Community

instance-scheduler

instance-scheduler really nice tool created by AWS Community Builder Mohamed Radwan that allows you to easily schedule stopping and starting your Amazon EC2 and RDS instances. He has put together this blog post, Create Instance Scheduler on Serverless by using Lambda, DynamoDB, API Gateway, Cognito,S3 and CloudFront to get you started.

libaws

libaws an interesting new tool from nathants that simplifies the provisioning and setting up of a number of AWS services. Defining what you need in YAML, you can then use the cli to build and deploy easily. Written in Go, check this out and see if this helps simplify how you create and delete infrastructure.

elasticspot

elasticspot this tool from Nick Figgins will help you simplify how to reassign Elastic IPs to new Spot instances with AWS Lambda and CloudWatch Events.

auto-close-aws-accounts

auto-close-aws-accounts this tool from Raajhesh Kannaa Chidambaram leverages the recently available API to close AWS accounts to enable you to automatically close AWS Accounts when moved to a specific Organizational Unit, such as SuspendedOU or ToBeDeletedOU or BlackHole.

Tools

amazon-ec2-spot-interrupter

amazon-ec2-spot-interrupter is a simple CLI tool that triggers Amazon EC2 Spot Interruption Notifications and Rebalance Recommendations. Detailed usage guide on how this tool works, this is a tool that you should add to your toolkit.

aws-glue-streaming-libs

aws-glue-streaming-libs this repo contains additions and enhancements to Spark Structured Streaming for ETL operations. This repository contains awsglue, a Python libary you can use to author AWS Glue Streaming ETL job that extends Apache Spark with additional data types and operations for Streaming ETL workflows, and bin a number of executables that allow you to run the Python library locally or open up a PySpark shell to run Glue Spark code interactively.

audit-plugin-for-mysql

audit-plugin-for-mysql this Audit Plugin for MySQL Server is used by Amazon RDS for MySQL to enable logging of server activity, typically for security and compliance purposes. Make sure you read the README about contributions and roadmap for this repo.

aws-lambda-powershell-runtime

aws-lambda-powershell-runtime provides the source code for the new PowerShell custom runtime for AWS Lambda that makes it even easier to run Lambda functions written in PowerShell to process events. Your code runs in an Amazon Linux environment that includes AWS credentials from an AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) role that you manage. Julian Wood has put together a blog post, Introducing the PowerShell custom runtime for AWS Lambda to provide a more detailed overview and a getting started guide.

automate-vending-sagemaker-notebooks-with-eventbridge-and-lambda

automate-vending-sagemaker-notebooks-with-eventbridge-and-lambda this is a super nice project that helps you automate how you can provision of SageMaker notebooks to your data scientists.

Demos, Samples and Workshops

amazon-chime-sdk-pstn-audio-workshop

amazon-chime-sdk-pstn-audio-workshop if you ever wanted to learn how to build telephony applications, then why not take a look at this workshop. This repo contains all the source code and workshop materials to help you do things like call recordings, call me back, call bridging, building a voice chat bot, call transcription and more. You can start your journey here.

gitlab-for-eks

gitlab-for-eks this repo contains a workshop that focuses specifically on using the GitLab Kubernetes Agent to accomplish integration of a GitLab Instance (including GitLab.com SaaS) with an EKS cluster for managing cluster applications that are built and tested by GitLab. Previous to the availability of the GitLab Kubernetes Agent, GitLab cluster integration was done via an SSL connection to the Kubernetes Control API endpoint for a cluster. All cluster deployment happened as a matter of CI pushes to the cluster API endpoint.

landing-zone-accelerator-on-aws

landing-zone-accelerator-on-aws this repo contains the Landing Zone Accelerator on AWS solution helps you quickly deploy a secure, resilient, scalable, and fully automated cloud foundation that accelerates your readiness for your cloud compliance program. A landing zone is a cloud environment that offers a recommended starting point, including default accounts, account structure, network and security layouts, and so forth. From a landing zone, you can deploy workloads that utilize your solutions and applications.

go-redis-apprunner

go-redis-apprunner this repo contains code using AWS CDK to package and deploy a sample app (along with infrastructure), and run it as an AWS App Runner service that integrates with MemoryDB for Redis. My colleague Abhishek Gupta has put together an accompanying guide to walk you through how this works, in the post Build Cloud-Native apps with AWS App Runner, Redis and AWS CDK

aws-graviton-ml-inference-armnn-example

aws-graviton-ml-inference-armnn-example provides sample code and steps you need to deploy ArmNN on AWS Lambda by building and loading a container image. The Lambda function will be used to efficiently perform ML inference (on Arm architecture) using an example image classification ML model in ONNX format (ResNet 50).

AWS and Community blog posts

OpenSearch

Great news for folks interested in OpenSearch as v2 officially launched earlier this week. Charlotte Henkle, Nicholas Knize, James McIntyre, Mohammad Qureshi, and Sean Zheng share more details in the post, OpenSearch 2.0 is now available!.

Steampipe

Following up from a previous post, Bob Tordella follows that up by showing you how you can now run a security audit from within AWS CloudShell in his post, How to perform a security audit of your AWS account in AWS CloudShell [hands on]

DAMON

I featured a project back in #86 called damo, a user space tool for DAMON (a data access monitoring framework subsystem for the Linux kernel). Using this, you can monitor the data access patterns of your system or workloads and make data access-aware memory management optimisations. I recently found out that this project was created by someone here at Amazon, SeongJae Park. You can catch up with the recent updates by checking out, DAMON: Data Access Monitor

Redis

Announced earlier this week, Redis application developers now have native support for JavaScript Object Notation (JSON) within ElastiCache and MemoryDB. In the post, Unlocking JSON workloads with ElastiCache and MemoryDB Allen Samuels, Joe Travaglini, and Joe Hu cover how JSON support works at a more technical level and provide examples of how you can write JSON documents, efficiently fetch or set portions of a JSON document in Redis. [hands on]

Inverting Proxy

Inverting Proxy is an open-source reverse proxy that inverts the direction of traffic between the proxy and the backend servers. In the post, Implementing lightweight on-premises API connectivity using inverting traffic proxy Oleksiy Volkov explores the use of lightweight application inversion proxy as a solution for multi-point hybrid or multi-cloud, API-level connectivity for cases where AWS Direct Connect or VPN may not be practical. This is super interesting, and I am wondering how else I might be able to use this tool. Essential reading this week! [hands on]

cdk-nag

cdk-nag (inspired by cfn_nag) validates that the state of constructs within a given scope comply with a given set of rules. Rule sets (NagPacks) includes things such as HIPAA Security, NIST 800-53 rev 4, NIST 800-53 rev 5, and PCI DSS 3.2.1. In the post, Manage application security and compliance with the AWS Cloud Development Kit and cdk-nag Arun Donti demonstrates how to integrate cdk-nag into an AWS CDK application to provide continual feedback and help align your applications with best practices. [hands on]

AWS Amplify and Flutter

The AWS Amplify Flutter Authenticator is a drop in UI library that allows Flutter developers to add a customisable authentication and registration quickly. Abdallah Shaban and Ashish Nanda have collaborated on this blog post, Setup sign-up and sign-in flows for your Flutter app in minutes with AWS Amplify which provides a hands on guide together with supporting source code. [hands on]

Other posts you might like from the past week

Case studies

Quick updates

Open 3D Engine

Earlier this week saw the release of 22.05.0 of Open 3D Engine (O3DE) an Apache 2.0-licensed open source project. O3DE is an engine for real-time 3D development that provides developers with a modular foundation for building games and 3D simulations across a variety of applications like robotics, digital twins, automotive, healthcare, and more. In our ongoing support of O3DE, the AWS Game Engine and Developer Services team contributed numerous features to version 22.05.0 including:

Read more about this release in the announcement, AWS for Games latest contribution to the Open 3D Engine (O3DE)

Amazon FSx for Lustre

Amazon FSx for Lustre, a service that provides cost-effective, high-performance, and scalable file storage for compute workloads, now supports root squash. This administrative feature adds an additional layer of file access control on top of the current network-based access control and POSIX file permissions that FSx for Lustre provides. Using the root squash feature, you can restrict root level file system access from clients that access an FSx for Lustre file system as root.

IT administrators often give users root access on their compute instances to perform privileged operations such as changing system configurations or installing and removing software. However, for instances attached to a Linux based file system, such as FSx for Lustre, a user who is granted root access also gets full access to files on the file system, which may not be desirable for certain scenarios. With root squash enabled, you can continue to give users root access to instances attached to an FSx for Lustre file system, while limiting file system permissions to those of a less-privileged user and group.

Redis

Amazon ElastiCache for Redis and Amazon MemoryDB for Redis now support natively storing and accessing data in the JavaScript Object Notation (JSON) format. With this launch, application developers can effortlessly store, fetch, and update their JSON data inside Redis without needing to manage custom code for serialisation and deserialisation. Using ElastiCache and MemoryDB, you can now efficiently retrieve and update specific portions of a JSON document without needing to manipulate the entire object, which can help improve performance and help reduce cost. You can also search your JSON document contents using the JSONPath query syntax. JSON support is available for Redis version 6.2 and above for ElastiCache and MemoryDB in all available regions, at no additional cost.

Grafana

Amazon Managed Grafana now supports a new API for creating Grafana API tokens, as well as support for new plugins, Grafana version 8.4, and workspace tags. With CreateWorkspaceApiKey, customers can create Grafana API tokens without having to log into the Grafana workspace console, enabling users to programmatically create, delete, and manage Grafana resources such as dashboards, alerts, and data sources. Amazon Managed Grafana adds support for Github, Moogsoft, Pixie, and Windrose plugins, enabling customers to connect, query, and visualize data from additional data sources. Existing and new Amazon Managed Grafana workspaces now support Grafana version 8.4, with no action required from users. Customers can now tag Amazon Managed Grafana workspaces to help simplify organisation and cost management of workspaces. Tags are labels in the form of key-value pairs that may be attached to Amazon Managed Grafana workspaces to search, filter, or allocate costs.

AWS Distro for OpenTelemetry

Earlier in the week was the announcement of the general availability of AWS Distro for OpenTelemetry (ADOT) for metrics, a secure, production-ready, AWS-supported distribution of the OpenTelemetry project. With this launch, customers can use OpenTelemetry APIs and SDKs in Java, .Net, and JavaScript to collect and send metrics to Amazon CloudWatch, Amazon Managed Service for Prometheus, and other monitoring destinations supported by the OpenTelemetry Protocol (OTLP). Part of the Cloud Native Computing Foundation (CNCF), OpenTelemetry provides open source APIs, libraries, and agents to collect distributed traces and metrics for application and infrastructure monitoring. With ADOT, you can instrument your applications just once to send metrics and traces to multiple monitoring solutions and use auto-instrumentation agents to collect traces and metrics without changing your code. Use AWS Distro for OpenTelemetry to instrument your applications running on Amazon Elastic Compute Cloud (EC2), Amazon Elastic Container Service (ECS), and Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (EKS).

For your observability needs, you can choose Amazon CloudWatch, Amazon Managed Service for Prometheus, or one of the AWS partner destinations. You can configure and deploy the latest version of the AWS Distro for OpenTelemetry for container services and Amazon EC2 by using AWS CloudFormation templates, the AWS Command Line Interface, Kubectl commands, or the ADOT EKS add-on. Developers can use the several supported receivers such as Prometheus as well as OpenTelemetry SDKs to collect or instrument their applications for collecting correlated metrics and traces.

Videos of the week

AWS Lambda Powertools

AWS Lambda Powertools is a suite of TypeScript utilities for AWS Lambda functions to ease adopting best practices such as tracing, structured logging, custom metrics, and more. Join Marcia Villalba and Sara Gerion as they dive deep into this project, getting hands on and showing you more about this tool. Extra bonus points as Sara uses Terraform throughout.

Kuma

Kuma is an open source service mesh that can run on every cloud, in a single or multi-zone capacity, across both Kubernetes and VMs. In this video, Kat Morgan walks you through how to install and run Kuma on AWS ECS

Events for your diary

BOSC 2022
July 13-14, Madison, Wisconsin, USA

The Bioinformatics Open Source Conference (BOSC) has been held annually since 2000, and this year AWS is proud to be a platinum sponsor for this event. BOSC covers all aspects of open source bioinformatics software and open science, including (but not limited to) these topics, Open Science and Reproducible Research, Open Biomedical Data, Citizen/Participatory Science, Standards and Interoperability, Data Science Workflows, Open Approaches to Translational Bioinformatics, Developer Tools and Libraries, Inclusion, and Outreach and Training. This is a hybrid event (in person/virtual) and you find out more by checking out the event page, BOSC 2022

OpenSearch
Every other Tuesday, 3pm GMT

This regular meet-up is for anyone interested in OpenSearch & Open Distro. All skill levels are welcome and they cover and welcome talks on topics including: search, logging, log analytics, and data visualisation.

Sign up to the next session, OpenSearch Community Meeting

OpenSearchCon 2022
Sept 21st, 2022 Seattle

Come to the first annual OpenSearchCon!

This day-long conference will be packed with presenters who build and innovate with OpenSearch. It doesn’t matter if you’re just getting started on your OpenSearch journey, running giant clusters, or contributing tons of code; the event is for everyone. Join us to celebrate the progress and look into the future of the project. Admission is free, and registration will be open in the next few weeks. All you will need to do is sign up, and get to Seattle!

Check out the full details, including signing up and location, at the meetup page here.

Stay in touch with open source at AWS

I hope this summary has been useful. Remember to check out the Open Source homepage to keep up to date with all our activity in open source by following us on @AWSOpen

item.title: 来自 MS Build 2022 的顶级 DevOps 公告 | Top DevOps Announcement from MS Build 2022
item.description:
今天我将回顾一下微软在 Build 2002 上发布的关于 DevOps 的所有最重要的公告

视频

像往常一样，如果你是一个视觉学习者，或者只是喜欢看和听而不是阅读，这里有完整的解释和演示视频，公平地说，它比这篇文章更完整。

视频链接：https://youtu.be/Lq0BPwa69C8

如果您更喜欢阅读，那么……让我们继续吧:)

伟大的缺席

让我们不要从公告开始，而是从缺乏公告开始，在 Build 2022 中大量缺席：我说的是 Azure DevOps。

事实上，有很多围绕 GitHub 的公告和会议，这是意料之中的，但没有一个会议、公告、功能或演示在其中展示甚至提到了 Azure DevOps。

我认为这对我们来说是一个明确的信号，表明 Azure DevOps 的未来会是什么样子，但请继续关注，因为我很快就会在我的YouTube 频道上迎来一位很棒的嘉宾，我们将确切地讨论这个问题。

谈论 Build 2022 中实际出现的内容，让我们从我最喜欢的公告之一开始：Azure 现在有一个完全托管的 nginx 产品。

紧密的 Azure 集成使用户只需单击几下即可通过 Azure 门户进行预配和配置。

该服务提供高级流量管理功能，例如 JSON Web 令牌 (JWT) 身份验证和主动健康检查，以及用于 SSL/TLS 证书管理的 Azure Key Vault 等内置安全集成。

最棒的是，作为一项 Azure 服务，您可以通过 Azure 门户管理它的所有方面，包括进行配置更改，然后直接应用于服务，以及使用之前部署中可能已经拥有的配置文件。

最后但同样重要的是，他们开发了一个 GitHub 集成，允许您对 GitHub 存储库中的配置进行版本控制，并通过 GitHub Actions 将更改应用于该配置

微软开发盒

我们将在一分钟内再次讨论容器，但让我换个页面谈谈我非常喜欢的另一个公告。 我说的是微软开发盒。

该服务基本上使开发人员可以轻松访问他们需要的工具和资源，而无需担心工作站配置和维护。

如果这听起来像 GitHub Codespaces 所做的，是因为在某种程度上，这两个服务具有相同的目的：使开发更容易并更快地管理依赖项。

然而，Dev Boxes 比 Codespaces 更进了一步，因为它支持各种开发，包括桌面和移动设备，并且可以在 Windows 11 上运行（而 codespaces 仅在 linux 上运行）。 事实上，您可以从浏览器中使用它，也可以使用远程桌面。

开发团队为特定项目和任务预配置开发盒，使开发人员能够快速开始使用准备好在几分钟内构建和运行应用程序的环境。 同时，Microsoft Dev Box 通过利用 Windows 365 将 Dev Boxes 与 Intune 和 Microsoft Endpoint Manager 集成，确保统一的管理、安全性和合规性掌握在 IT 手中。

当您准备好测试您的应用程序或针对其他服务运行它们时，Azure 部署环境（也是私人预览版）将使开发人员团队可以轻松地通过基于项目的基础架构的自助服务快速启动应用基础架构。代码模板。

这将最大限度地减少设置时间，同时最大限度地提高安全性、合规性和成本效率，并实施最佳实践

现在让我们转到下一个新服务 Azure 负载测试。

几年前，微软在 Azure DevOps 中集成了自己的负载测试服务……实际上，当时它仍然被称为 Visual Studio Team Services。 但该服务已被弃用多年，导致 Microsoft 和 Azure 没有适当的负载测试服务……再也没有了。

它使开发人员和测试人员能够生成大规模负载并运行模拟，从而揭示对应用程序性能、可扩展性和容量的可行见解。

该服务可以使用现有的 Apache JMeter 脚本，并且您将获得由指标和分析支持的建议。

我真的很高兴尝试这项服务，请继续关注，因为我肯定会有一个视频，我将在其中测试和审查它。

好的，接下来是 Azure 容器应用的 GA。 该服务已经预览了一段时间，但现在它已达到 GA，这意味着它对于生产工作负载来说是稳定的。

所有这一切都由 Microsoft 完全管理，因此您可以只专注于应用程序而不是管理基础架构

该服务的一个很酷的功能是，您可以将 pod 缩减到 0 个实例，并在收到请求时自动启动更多 pod。

如果这一切还不够，在活动期间，Scott Guthrie 展示了一个演示，其中通过 Azure CLI 的单个命令，他能够将应用程序容器化，在 Azure 中创建容器注册表，创建并运行一些 GitHub Actions 工作流构建应用程序的映像，最后在全新的 Azure 容器应用程序中将所有内容部署到 Azure。

草案 v2 集成

如果您对应用程序如何容器化感到好奇，这要感谢 Build 2022 上发布的另一项新公告。我说的是对 Draft v2 支持的公开预览。

随着刚刚宣布的公开预览版，Draft 现在通过 Azure CLI、Azure 门户和 Visual Studio Code 与 AKS 集成。 正如我们所见，它也适用于 Azure 容器应用程序。

仍然在谈论容器和 AKS，在 Build 上宣布了另一个有趣的功能：Web App Routing 的预览。

我找不到太多关于这个新的 AKS 插件的信息，而且发布公告的部分会话也很短，但我们从官方网站上了解到的是

我很想看到它的实际效果，如果您已经尝试过，请在评论中告诉我。

完全托管的 Grafana

我今天要介绍的下一个也是最后一个公告对我来说是另一个重要的公告。 微软宣布在 Azure 中公开预览完全托管的 Grafana 服务。

正如我所提到的，Azure Managed Grafana 是一种用于分析和监控解决方案的完全托管服务。 它由提供可扩展数据可视化的 Grafana Enterprise 提供支持。

你可以预配新的 Azure Managed Grafana 并将其连接到 AKS 群集，这样你就可以在 Grafana 熟悉的界面中查看容器洞察力提供的所有指标和数据。 您可以查看每秒请求数、用于 pod 的资源以及成功运行应用程序所需的所有不同信息等信息。

由于 Grafana 是一种开源产品，因此您可以在 Azure 或任何您想运行它的地方使用它。

是的，您在上面看到的仪表板是在您预配 Azure Managed Grafana 并将其指向 AKS 时自动创建的，因为该服务针对来自 Azure Monitor 和 Azure Data Explorer 等服务的 Azure 本机数据源进行了优化。

结论

对我来说，这些是 Build 2022 上围绕 DevOps 最激动人心的公告，但微软发布了更多公告，因此我建议您查看他们发布的新闻之书以查看所有内容。 您可以在视频说明中找到链接。

请在下面的评论中告诉我您对我介绍的服务和新功能有何看法，以及您最喜欢的 Build 2022 公告是什么。

另外，请在此处查看此视频，其中我回顾了一种无需 VPN 即可连接到私有资源的新方法。

喜欢，分享和关注我🚀以获取更多内容：

📽 YouTube 📽 YouTube
☕ Buy me a coffee ☕给我买杯咖啡
💖 Patreon 💖 Patreon
📧 Newsletter 📧时事通讯
🌐 CoderDave.io Website 🌐 CoderDave.io 网站
👕 Merch 👕 商品
👦🏻 Facebook page 👦🏻脸书专页
🐱‍💻 GitHub 🐱‍💻 GitHub
👲🏻 Twitter 👲🏻推特
👴🏻 LinkedIn 👴🏻领英
🔉 Podcast 🔉播客



原文:

Microsoft Build 2022 has just closed its virtual doors, with a lot of new services and features, and this time around a lot has been said also about DevOps.

Today I’m going to recap all the most important announcements around DevOps made by Microsoft at Build 2002

Video

As usual, if you are a visual learner, or simply prefer to watch and listen instead of reading, here you have the video with the whole explanation and demo, which to be fair is much more complete than this post.

Link to the video: https://youtu.be/Lq0BPwa69C8

If you rather prefer reading, well... let's just continue :)

The Great Absent

Let’s start not with an announcement but rather with a lack thereof, with the great absent at Build 2022: I’m talking about Azure DevOps.

There were in fact a lot of announcements and sessions revolving around GitHub, and that’s to be expected, but not a single session, announcement, feature or demo in which Azure DevOps has been shown or even mentioned.

I think this is a clear sign for us on what the future for Azure DevOps looks like, but stay tuned because I will have a great guest on my YouTube channel very soon and we will talk exactly about this.

Fully Managed NGNIX on Azure

Talking about stuff that was actually present at Build 2022, let’s start with one of my favorite announcements: Azure now has a fully managed nginx offering.

NGINX for Azure, which is currently in preview, is a SaaS solution with advanced traffic management and monitoring. The tight Azure integration enables ease of use with a few clicks for provisioning and configuration though the Azure portal.

The service offers advanced traffic management features, such as JSON Web Token (JWT) authentication and active health checks, and built-in security integrations like Azure Key Vault for SSL/TLS certificate management.

NGINX for Azure is offered by Microsoft in partnership with F5, so you can find it through the Azure Marketplace, but you get a unified bill for all services they use on Azure and leverage existing enterprise agreements.

And the great thing is that being an Azure service, you can manage all the aspects of it via the Azure portal, including making configurations changes that are then applied to the service directly, and using config files that you may already have from previous deployments.

And last but not least, they have developed a GitHub integration that allows you to version control your configuration in a GitHub repo and to apply changes to that configuration via GitHub Actions

Microsoft Dev Boxes

We will talk again about containers in a minute, but let me change page and talk about another announcement I quite liked. I’m talking about Microsoft Dev Boxes.

Microsoft Dev Boxes, currently in private preview, is a new cloud service that provides developers with secure, ready-to-code developer workstations. This service basically makes it easy for developers to access the tools and resources they need without worrying about workstation configuration and maintenance.

If it sounds like what GitHub Codespaces does is because, in a way, the two services have the same purpose: make development easier and management of dependencies quicker.

Dev Boxes, however, goes a step further than Codespaces, because it supports all kinds of development, including desktop and mobile, and it runs on Windows 11 (while codespaces run only on linux). And in fact you can use it from the browser but also using Remote Desktop.

Dev teams preconfigure Dev Boxes for specific projects and tasks, enabling devs to get started quickly with an environment that’s ready to build and run their app in minutes. At the same time, Microsoft Dev Box ensures unified management, security, and compliance stay in the hands of IT by leveraging Windows 365 to integrate Dev Boxes with Intune and Microsoft Endpoint Manager.

Azure Deployment Environments

And when you are ready to test your applications or run them against other services, Azure Deployment Environments, also in private preview, will make it easy for developer teams to quickly spin up app infrastructure with self-service of project-based infrastructure-as-code templates.

This will minimize set-up time while maximizing security, compliance, and cost efficiency, and enforcing best practices

Azure Load Testing

Let’s move now to the next new service, Azure Load Testing.

Few years ago, Microsoft had their own Load Testing service integrated in Azure DevOps… actually, at that time it was still called Visual Studio Team Services. But that service has been deprecated for years already, leaving Microsoft and Azure without a proper Load Testing service… well no more.

Azure Load Testing is a fully managed load-testing service built for and in Azure that makes it easy to generate huge load using Azure resources and identify app performance bottlenecks.

It enables developers and testers to generate high-scale load and run simulations that reveal actionable insights into app performance, scalability, and capacity.

The service can use existing Apache JMeter scripts, and you will get recommendations backed by metrics and analytics.

Azure Load Testing also supports continuous integration and continuous delivery (CI/CD) workflows to give continuous feedback.

I’m really excited to try this service out, stay tuned because I will for sure have a video in which I’m going to test it and review it.

Azure Container Apps GA

Alright, next up the GA of Azure Container Apps. The service has been in preview for a while, but now it has reached GA which means that it’s stable for production workloads.

Azure Container Apps allows you to run your containerized apps in a serverless manner, but still taking advantages of Kubernetes, Keda for event-driven autoscaling, dapr, and Envoy.

And all of this is fully managed by Microsoft so you can just focus on the application and not on managing infrastructure

One of the cool features of this service is that you can scale your pods down to 0 instances and spin up more pods automatically if requests come in.

And if all of this was not enough, during the event Scott Guthrie showed a demo in which with a single command of the Azure CLI he was able to have an application containerized, a container registry create in Azure, some GitHub Actions workflows created and run to build the image of the application, and finally have everything deployed to Azure in a brand new Azure Container App.

Draft v2 Integration

And if you are curious about how the app is getting containerized, this is thanks to another new announcement made at Build 2022. I’m talking about the public preview of the support for Draft v2.

Draft is an open-source project that streamlines Kubernetes development by taking a non-containerized application and generating the Dockerfiles, Kubernetes manifests, Helm charts, Kustomize configurations, and other artifacts associated with a containerized application. Draft can also create a GitHub Action workflow file to quickly build and deploy applications onto any Kubernetes cluster.

With the public preview just announced, Draft is now integrated with AKS through the Azure CLI, Azure Portal, and Visual Studio Code. And works with Azure Container Apps as well, as we have seen.

AKS Web App Routing

And still talking about containers and AKS, there is another interesting feature that has been announced at Build: the preview for Web App Routing.

I couldn’t find much about this new AKS add-on, and also the part of the session where the announcement has been made was quite short, but what we know from the official website is that

Web app routing helps you get your web application up and running in AKS securely while removing the complexity of ingress controller, certificate, and DNS management, and that the service offers a managed ingress controller powered by NGINX that you can use without restrictions and integrates out of the box with open service mesh to secure intra-cluster communications.

I’m curious to see this in action, let me know in the comments if you have already tried this out.

Fully Managed Grafana

Next and final announcement that I’m going to cover today is another big one for me. Microsoft announced the public preview of a fully managed Grafana service in Azure.

As I’ve mentioned, Azure Managed Grafana is a fully managed service for analytics and monitoring solutions. And it's supported by Grafana Enterprise, which provides extensible data visualizations.

You can provision the new Azure Managed Grafana and connect it to your AKS cluster, and with that you can see all the metrics and data provided by container insights, visualized in the familiar interface of Grafana. You can see information like requests per second, the resources that you're using for your pods, and all of the different things you need to run your application successfully.

And since Grafana is an open-source product, it's available to you in Azure or wherever you want to run it.

And yes, that dashboard you see above is automatically created when you provision Azure Managed Grafana and point it to AKS because the service is optimized for Azure-native data sources from services such as Azure Monitor and Azure Data Explorer.

Conclusions

These were for me the most exciting announcements around DevOps at Build 2022, but Microsoft made a lot more announcements so I’d recommend you to check the Book of News that they published to see them all. You can find the link in the video description.

Let me know in the comments below what do you think about the services and new features I’ve covered and what your favorite announcement from Build 2022 was.

Also, check out this video here in which I review a new way to connect to private resources without VPN.

Like, share and follow me 🚀 for more content:

📽 YouTube
☕ Buy me a coffee
💖 Patreon
📧 Newsletter
🌐 CoderDave.io Website
👕 Merch
👦🏻 Facebook page
🐱‍💻 GitHub
👲🏻 Twitter
👴🏻 LinkedIn
🔉 Podcast
item.author: n3wt0n

item.title: Pantangan Setelah 填料 | Pantangan Setelah Filler
item.description:
琼脂 mendapatkan hasil seperti yang diinginkan，ada beberapa hal yang menjadi pantangan setelah 填料。 阿帕萨哈？ 皮肤填充剂 banyak digemari orang karena salah satu alasan, yaitu aman。 琼脂 tidak merusak hasil perawatan 填料，sebaiknya kamu juga tidak melakukan ciuman terlebih dahulu。



原文:

Pantangan Setelah Filler Dagu – Saat ini, Derma Filler merupakan sebuah perawatan yang sangat banyak digemari oleh orang, terutama wanita. Hal ini disebabkan karena perawatan filler, dianggap memberikan hasil yang sangat baik bagi wajah. Khususnya bagi mereka yang ingin tetap tampil dengan awet muda.

Biasanya orang melakukan suntik derma filler bertujuan untuk menyamarkan kerutan dan garis halus di wajah. Selain itu, derma filler juga dapat diupayakan untuk meratakan tekstur pada kulit, menghaluskan kulit, dan menghilangkan bekas luka yang ada pada kulit. Agar mendapatkan hasil seperti yang diinginkan, ada beberapa hal yang menjadi pantangan setelah filler. Apa saja? Berikut adalah ulasannya.

**Sekilas Informasi Mengenai Suntik Derma Filler **Suntik filler adalah sebuah perawatan yang dilakukan untuk memperbaiki atau mengoreksi area tertentu yang dirasa memang membutuhkan. Pada tindakan prosedurnya, filler dilakukan dengan metode injeksi, dan biasanya dokter membutuhkan waktu kurang lebih 30 menit untuk menyelesaikan prosedur ini. Hasil injeksi dari filler biasanya bertahan sekitar enam bulan hingga satu tahun lamanya. Pada masa ini, kamu sebaiknya juga harus mengetahui tentang pantangan setelah filler dagu. Dermal filler banyak digemari orang karena salah satu alasan, yaitu aman. Sebab, perawatan ini hanya boleh dilakukan oleh dokter spesialis kulit, dokter bedah kecantikan, atau terapis kecantikan yang kompeten. Lebih spesifik, dokter yang menangani filler juga harus memiliki sertifikat ahli dalam prosedur kecantikan ini.

Suntik filler mungkin akan menimbulkan beberapa efek samping di area suntikan seperti kemerahan, bengkak, nyeri, gatal, dan infeksi. Selain itu, efek samping lain dari filler adalah reaksi alergi, emboli pembuluh darah, perpindahan filler, dan luka jaringan parut (keloid). Oleh karena itu, konsultasikan terlebih dahulu dengan dokter Anda sebelum melakukan filler. Hal ini dilakukan untuk menghindari atau mengurangi efek samping dan risiko yang mungkin terjadi.

**Pantangan setelah melakukan suntik filler
**Walaupun Derma Filler merupakan sebuah perawatan yang aman, namun pasien yang melakukan filler memiliki beberapa pantangan yang harus diperhatikan. Pantangan ini, akan kami jelaskan menurut pada area yang biasanya dilakukan injeksi. Berikut adalah penjelasan lebih lengkapnya:

**Pantangan Setelah Filler Dagu
**Setelah melakukan injeksi filler dagu, dokter biasanya akan memberi saran agar pasien tidak menyentuh, memencet, meminjat, atau menggosok area dagu ataupun sekitarnya. Sebisa mungkin, terapkan hal ini setidaknya satu hari setelah suntik filler dilakukan. Hal ini bertujuan untuk mencegah kerusakan dan terjadinya perpindahan filler yang telah disuntikkan.

Kamu juga tidak dianjurkan untuk melakukan perawatan pada area wajah, setidaknya dua hari setelah prosedur suntik filler dilakukan. Hindari juga memakai produk perawatan kecantikan apa pun atau menggunakan riasan pada area wajah setelah melakukan suntik filler.

Selain hal yang telah disebutkan di atas, kamu juga tidak dibolehkan untuk melakukan aktivitas berat. Contohnya seperti berolahraga, berhubungan seks, ataupun aktivitas fisik lain yang memerlukan tenaga ekstra. Hal ini disebabkan karena setiap aktivitas yang meningkatkan tekanan darah dan detak jantung, dapat membuat atau menimbulkan memar.

Pasien derma filler sebaiknya juga menghindari minum alkohol dan berbagai minuman yang mengandung kafein. Selain itu, hindari juga makanan yang tinggi natrium (garam), gula, dan karbohidrat, serta makanan pedas. Tujuan dari pantangan setelah filler dagu ini, berupaya agar tidak terjadi kerusakan pada area tindakan. Dengan lebih berhati-hati, perawatan derma filler yang kamu lakukan pun akan membuat hasil yang lebih maksimal.

**Pantangan setelah filler bibir
**Jika kamu melakukan suntik filler pada area bibir, ada beberapa pantangan yang juga harus kamu perhatikan. Salah satunya adalah aktivitas yang menimbulkan panas pada area bibir dan dapat merusak hasil filler. Oleh sebab itu, dianjurkan bagi pasien untuk tidak merokok, minum minuman yang terlalu panas, dan sauna setelah melakukan injeksi filler.

Selain itu, kamu juga disarankan untuk tidak menggunakan lipstik atau berbagai produk kosmetik bibir lainnya. Setidaknya, lakukan anjuran ini setelah satu hari melakukan suntik filler. Agar tidak merusak hasil perawatan filler, sebaiknya kamu juga tidak melakukan ciuman terlebih dahulu.
